275	### Known unknown — `workflow_dispatch` from a non-default branch
276	
277	**The constraint:** Per GitHub Actions docs and confirmed by Epic 1 Story 1.2 execution (`epic-1-retro § Patterns across all three stories → Task spec drifted from actual API/UI`), **`workflow_dispatch` fires ONLY if the workflow file exists on the default branch.** Story 3.2 dispatches against `feat/release-workflow`, and `release.yaml` does NOT exist on `main` yet (Story 3.3's scope).
278	
279	**The primary attempt** (`gh workflow run release.yaml --ref feat/release-workflow -f version_bump=alpha`) may succeed because `gh` invokes the REST API directly, and GitHub's constraint has been loosened in some versions — but empirically we hit the strict interpretation in Story 1.2. The dev agent MUST try primary first; if it fails, fallback.
280	
281	**The fallback:** replicate the Story 1.2 pattern — add a temporary `on: push: branches: [feat/release-workflow]` trigger to `release.yaml`. The next push to that branch fires the workflow automatically. Story 3.2's Task 6 reverts the trigger before Story 3.3 opens its merge PR so `release.yaml` on `main` has ONLY `workflow_dispatch`.
282	
283	**Why not just merge `release.yaml` to `main` first?** Two reasons: (a) merging a workflow that has NEVER been dispatched is the scope Story 3.2 was created to gate — we would lose the "test before main" safety; (b) Story 3.3 AC #1 is explicit about Story 3.2 preceding the merge. The sequence epic design is load-bearing.
284	
285	**Why not use a caller workflow (`workflow_call`)?** It's cleaner in the abstract but requires a deeper edit to `release.yaml` (adding `on: workflow_call`) which complicates Story 3.3's merge diff. The transient push-trigger + revert is surgical and matches the Epic 1 Story 1.2 precedent. Use it if primary fails.
286	
287	**If BOTH paths fail unexpectedly:** STOP. Document the symptom in Dev Agent Record. Escalate — do NOT ad-hoc attempt a third mechanism (e.g., force-pushing a throwaway release.yaml to `main`) under pressure. The whole point of this story is to create auditable evidence, not artifacts of desperation.
288	
289	### Release env allow-list — strict widen/revoke discipline
290	
291	The `release` environment's `deployment_branch_policy` is `custom_branch_policies: true` with allow-list `{main}` only (Story 1.2 invariant). A `workflow_dispatch` (or push-trigger) against any other branch is rejected with the "branch not allowed" error — the `environment: release` binding engages before step-1 even runs.
292	
293	Story 3.2 is the ONLY story in the current epic that legitimately needs to add a non-`main` entry to that list. The canonical pattern is `docs/RELEASING.md § Temporarily allowing a feature branch:132–153` (itself written with Story 3.2 in mind). The two-call shape is:
294	
295	```bash
296	# Widen
297	POLICY_ID=$(gh api --method POST \
298	  repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/environments/release/deployment-branch-policies \
299	  --input - --jq .id <<'JSON'
300	{ "name": "feat/release-workflow" }
301	JSON
302	)
303	
304	# ... run the validation cut, approve via UI, verify publish ...
305	
306	# Revoke immediately
307	gh api --method DELETE \
308	  "repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/environments/release/deployment-branch-policies/$POLICY_ID"
309	```
310	
311	**Failure modes & mitigations:**
312	
313	- **`POST` returns `422 already_exists`** — the branch is already on the list (possibly from an aborted prior Story 3.2 attempt). Fetch the existing id via GET + jq and treat that as POLICY_ID for the revoke.
314	- **`POLICY_ID` is lost mid-session** (shell crash, network hiccup, context compaction) — Task 2 persists the id into `/tmp/release-env-policy-id.txt` as a backup. If even that's gone, fetch via GET and filter by name.
315	- **Revoke runs BEFORE the publish completes** (dev-agent ordering error) — the env gate fails on the resume; workflow aborts; no publish happens. Non-destructive; re-run the widen + dispatch.
316	- **Revoke is skipped entirely** — the allow-list has a trailing hole until the next maintainer notices. AC #8 treats this as a blocking defect; Story 3.2 is NOT complete until `deployment-branch-policies` returns to `{main}` only.
317	
318	### Expected run shape — blow-by-blow
319	
320	Based on Story 3.1 commit `34ad130`'s `release.yaml`, the alpha-cut run SHALL execute ~14 steps in this order (step names match `release.yaml` exactly):
321	
322	| # | Step | Runtime (est.) | Expected output |
323	|---|------|---------------:|-----------------|
324	| 1 | Checkout | <10s | `feat/release-workflow` at `34ad130` (or defect-fix HEAD) checked out with `fetch-depth: 0` |
325	| 2 | Setup Node.js | <30s | Node 22.x (from `.nvmrc`) installed + npm cache primed |
326	| 3 | Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing | <20s | `npm install -g npm@latest` → `npm --version` ≥ 11.5.1 |
327	| 4 | Install uv | <15s | `uv` installed via `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6` (Python 3.12) |
328	| 5 | Install dependencies | <1m | `npm ci` — lockfile-driven install; `conventional-changelog-cli@5.0.0` resolves |
329	| 6 | Run tests and validation | ~2–4m | `npm test` — full 12 subcommands (prettier, eslint, markdownlint, validate:schemas, validate:refs, test:unit, test:agent-bmad, test:agent-brainstorming, test:integration, test:skill-rules, test:python). Matches `quality.yaml` matrix logic |
330	| 7 | **Pause at `environment: release` gate** | ≤5m (human wait) | "Review deployments" prompt in UI; approver clicks → resume |
331	| 8 | Configure Git | <1s | `github-actions[bot]` identity set |
332	| 9 | Bump version | <5s | `npm version prerelease --preid=alpha` on `0.10.0` → `0.10.1-alpha.0`; `package.json` + `package-lock.json` updated in-runner |
333	| 10 | Get new version and previous tag | <1s | outputs: `new_version=0.10.1-alpha.0`, `previous_tag=v0.10.0` |
334	| 11 | Update marketplace.json version | <1s | `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` `"version": "0.10.1-alpha.0"` |
335	| 12 | Update CHANGELOG.md | <5s | `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s` — **append behavior is the AC #10 observation target** |
336	| 13 | Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG | <1s | `grep -iE '(closes\|fixes\|resolves) \[#[^0-9]'` empty → exit 0 |
337	| 14 | Pre-publish dry-run | ~10s | `npm publish --dry-run` with `NPM_TOKEN: ""` — tarball manifest printed; exits 0 |
338	| 15 | Commit version bump | <1s | ephemeral commit `release: bump to v0.10.1-alpha.0` on runner HEAD |
339	| 16 | Generate release notes | <2s | `git log v0.10.0..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse` categorized into `### New Features` / `### Bug Fixes` / `### Other Changes` / `### Maintenance` |
340	| 17 | Create and push tag | ~5s | `git tag -a v0.10.1-alpha.0 -m ...` + `git push origin v0.10.1-alpha.0` → tag visible in GitHub tags list (pointing to the ephemeral bump commit) |
341	| 18 | Push commit to main | SKIPPED | `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` false on feature-branch dispatch |
342	| 19 | Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref) | <1s | `::notice::Dispatched from refs/heads/feat/release-workflow ...` — proof the Story 3.1 patch D1 path is live |
343	| 20 | Publish to npm via OIDC | ~15–30s | `npm publish --tag alpha` (OIDC handshake, no `NPM_TOKEN`, no `--provenance` flag) — success: provenance auto-attached |
344	| 21 | Create GitHub Release | ~5s | `softprops/action-gh-release@v2` creates `v0.10.1-alpha.0` release with `prerelease: true` |
345	| 22 | Summary | <1s | `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` block — header, npm URL, GitHub URL, provenance line |
346	
347	**Total wall-clock estimate:** 5–10 min CI runtime + human-approval wait (NFR1 target: <15 min CI + <5 min approval).
348	
349	### Load-bearing decisions — resolved at story-level
350	
351	**(D1) Dispatch mechanism — primary vs fallback.** Task 3 runs the primary `gh workflow run --ref` first. Fallback only if primary fails. Decision point at execution time, NOT at story-authoring time, because GHA's `workflow_dispatch` constraint behavior has shifted across GitHub's release cycle and the dev agent's live probe is the ground truth.
352	
353	**(D2) POLICY_ID persistence path.** Shell-var-only is brittle across session boundaries (compaction, network, manual context shifts). Task 2 writes the id to `/tmp/release-env-policy-id.txt` as a durable backup before the run. Task 6 reads either source; either works.
354	
355	**(D3) Defect-fix commits stay on `feat/release-workflow`, NOT on `main` or a new branch.** Any `release.yaml` fixes Story 3.2 needs to ship to validate are simply new commits on `feat/release-workflow`. Story 3.3's merge PR will carry them to `main` along with the Story 3.1 baseline. This keeps the branch-topology clean and the merge audit trail single-stranded.
356	
357	**(D4) Do NOT `npm deprecate` the test alpha unless a real problem is found.** The version `0.10.1-alpha.0` is a throwaway — even if it ships with minor cosmetics (e.g., a weird CHANGELOG shape from the first append-mode run), it is not a consumer-facing version (no one will `npm install bmad-module-skill-forge@alpha` intentionally yet, and `latest` still points at `0.10.0`). `npm deprecate` on this version is reserved for genuine defects uncovered in Task 5 (e.g., broken tarball, provenance absent, wrong dist-tag). Low-effort, reversible decision — take it at Task 7 close-out.
358	
359	### Coupling with past and future stories
360	
361	- **Story 1.1 (done, ruleset `13855503`)** — main branch protection is NOT touched by Story 3.2. The alpha cut pushes a tag (not a commit to `main`), which is not gated by the ruleset.
362	- **Story 1.2 (done, env `14347249917`)** — the env gate is the approval point exercised here. Story 3.2 temporarily modifies ONLY the env's `deployment_branch_policies` (allow-list widen + revoke) — reviewers, `prevent_self_review`, and secret-scope invariants are NOT touched.
363	- **Story 1.3 (done, npm TP registered 2026-04-20)** — first live exercise of the TP is Story 3.2's publish step. If the TP binding is miskeyed (e.g., case mismatch, `.yml` typo, env name drift), the publish 404s and AC #11's three-way comparison recipe diagnoses the specific field.
364	- **Story 2.1 (done, `conventional-changelog-cli@^5`)** — first live CI run of the tool happens here (Story 2.2's seed ran locally). If the tool errors on Linux CI (different from local dev), Story 3.2 catches it.
365	- **Story 2.2 (done, seeded CHANGELOG)** — Story 3.2's append is the first mutation of the seeded file via the tool. AC #10's lifecycle observation is the whole reason Story 3.1 deferred this to Story 3.2.
366	- **Story 2.3 (done, STABILITY.md DRAFT)** — not touched by Story 3.2. Story 5.1 flips DRAFT → STABLE.
367	- **Story 3.1 (done, `release.yaml` + 5 patches)** — Story 3.2 is the first consumer of the 5 review-applied patches. If any patch is load-bearing for Story 3.2 and breaks (e.g., the `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` gate, the pre-publish dry-run, the widened bogus-ref grep, the `github.server_url`/`github.repository` substitution), Story 3.2 surfaces it.
368	- **Story 3.3 (direct downstream consumer)** — Story 3.3 AC #1 reads "Given Story 3.2 validation has succeeded". Story 3.3 is BLOCKED until Story 3.2 Status → `done`. Story 3.3's merge PR will carry `feat/release-workflow` → `main`, which at that point contains Story 1.x docs + Story 2.x content + Story 3.1 workflow + any Story 3.2 defect-fix commits, minus the orphaned `release: bump to v0.10.1-alpha.0` commit (which lives only via the alpha tag ref).
369	- **Story 3.3 (concurrent carry-forward)** — Epic 1 carry-forward #2 is Story 3.3's scope: atomic neutralization of `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag trigger in the same PR that merges `release.yaml` to `main`. Story 3.2 is NOT touching `publish.yaml` at all.
370	- **Story 4.1 (backlog)** — six-scenario rollback playbook. Story 3.2 uses the playbook SKELETON from research doc lines 927–978 as the interim reference for defect response (AC #11). Story 3.1's `docs/RELEASING.md § Rollback Playbook` is a deliberate empty-header placeholder awaiting Story 4.1.
371	- **Story 5.1 (backlog, pre-v1.0.0 audit)** — Story 3.2's evidence block (workflow run URL, publish timestamp, provenance payload) is a precondition artifact Story 5.1 AC #1 references: "`release.yaml` merged to `main` (Story 3.3) and tested via at least one successful alpha cut (Story 3.2)". Story 3.2's Dev Agent Record is the audit-trail link.
372	- **Story 5.2 (backlog, v1.0.0-rc.1 cut)** — Story 3.2's alpha-cut pattern is rehearsed here; Story 5.2 repeats the same mechanics but with `version_bump: rc` and the one-time hand-bump of `package.json` version to `1.0.0-rc.0` first (per `deferred-work.md § 3-1-review "Story 5.2 needs a one-time hand-bump path"`).
373	- **Story 6.x (backlog, post-v1.0.0 cleanup)** — orthogonal. Story 6.1 deletes `publish.yaml` + `manual-release.yaml`; Story 6.3 removes `NPM_TOKEN` from repo secrets. All happen after Story 5.4 closes v1.0.0 green.
374	
375	### Epic 1 / Epic 2 retrospective carry-forwards in scope
376	
377	From `epic-1-retro-2026-04-20.md` and `epic-2-retro-2026-04-21.md`:
378	
379	| Carry-forward | Where applied in Story 3.2 |
380	|---|---|
381	| **Live `gh api` recon block at Task 1** (Epic 1 #4) | Applied — Task 1 has 11 probes (branch/workflow state, env state, TP state, package state, npm state, CHANGELOG state, tag state) |
382	| **Adversarial code review on every story** (Epic 1 #5) | Applicable at review-time, not story-authoring time. Flagged so reviewer knows to apply Blind/Edge/Auditor lenses |
383	| **Epic 1 Carry-forward #1 (`.yaml` not `.yml`)** | Applied in AC #1's filename verification; Task 1 probe. Story 3.2 is the live validator for the TP binding — a filename defect surfaces here as a 404 |
384	| **Epic 1 Carry-forward #3 (flip `prevent_self_review` on second reviewer)** | Standing flag; Story 3.2 operates under `prevent_self_review: false` (solo-maintainer approval is permitted by design) |
385	| **Epic 2 Carry-forward (B) `[Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle** | Applied in AC #10 — Story 3.2 is the DESIGNATED live validator for the preset's append behavior on the seeded file with the Story 3.1 Task 4 anchor. `deferred-work.md § 3-1-review "## [Unreleased] anchor lifecycle after first release is unverified"` explicitly names Story 3.2 |
386	| **Epic 2 Carry-forward (C) bogus-issue-ref guard** | Applied — Story 3.1 embedded the `grep -iE '(closes\|fixes\|resolves) \[#[^0-9]'` assertion (`release.yaml:91-96`); Story 3.2's publish run is the first post-guard release and proves the guard either catches or stays silent as designed |
387	| **Epic 2 Carry-forward (E) Windows smoke** | Applied in Story 3.1's `quality.yaml` edit, NOT in Story 3.2's territory. Noted for context only |
388	| **Epic 2 "When a downstream Given premise breaks mid-epic, patch the downstream spec in the current story's review"** (process improvement #7) | Flagged for Story 3.2 reviewer: if the alpha cut surfaces behavior that breaks Story 3.3's or Story 5.1's AC `Given` framing, patch that story's spec in Story 3.2's review, not just flag in Dev Notes |
389	
390	### Testing standards summary
391	
392	**No new automated tests are written in this story.** Story 3.2 is a live-validation story; the "test" IS the alpha-cut run. Verification is:
393	
394	1. **Workflow run** — GitHub Actions run URL is green, end-to-end.
395	2. **npm side** — `npm view` commands return the expected shape (AC #6).
396	3. **GitHub Release side** — `gh release view` returns the expected shape (AC #7).
397	4. **CHANGELOG side** — diff comparison answers the three AC #10 questions.
398	5. **Scope envelope** — `git diff 34ad130 feat/release-workflow -- .github/workflows/release.yaml` is empty at Story 3.2 close-out (modulo any intentional defect-fix commits).
399	6. **Allow-list state** — `gh api .../deployment-branch-policies` returns `{main}` only.
400	7. **Evidence completeness** — Dev Agent Record § Debug Log References populated per AC #13.

file.content:
# Story 3.2: Validate `release.yaml` via alpha test cut from feature branch

Status: ready-for-dev

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As the maintainer,
I want the `release.yaml` workflow (authored in Story 3.1, `feat/release-workflow` HEAD `34ad130`) exercised end-to-end on a throwaway alpha version **before it touches `main`**,
so that OIDC trusted publishing is proven working, the SLSA-L2 provenance attestation is verified attached on npm, the `release` environment approval gate fires, the dist-tag routing is proven `alpha → npm tag alpha` (NOT `latest`), and the CHANGELOG append + `[Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle is observed against real output — eliminating the known unknowns before Story 3.3 merges the workflow to `main` and before the v1.0.0 critical path in Epic 5 ever loads.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Precondition audit — Story 3.1 / 1.2 / 1.3 outputs verified intact before dispatch.** Reviewer SHALL confirm at execution time:
   - `.github/workflows/release.yaml` exists on `feat/release-workflow` (literal filename, extension `.yaml` not `.yml` — Epic 1 retro carry-forward #1, enforced character-for-character by the npm Trusted Publisher).
   - `feat/release-workflow` HEAD is at commit `34ad130` (Story 3.1 + 5 code-review patches) or later; no unrelated commits have landed between Story 3.1 close-out and Story 3.2 dispatch.
   - The `release` GitHub Environment (id `14347249917`, Story 1.2) has `armelhbobdad` as a required reviewer, `prevent_self_review: false`, and **zero** env-scoped secrets. `NPM_TOKEN` remains at repo scope, NOT env scope.
   - The npm Trusted Publisher (Story 1.3, registered 2026-04-20) carries the four fields verbatim: `armelhbobdad` / `bmad-module-skill-forge` / **`release.yaml`** / **`release`**. Inspection is UI-only per `docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher` (no public API).
   - `release.yaml` has NOT been merged to `main` yet (Story 3.3 owns that merge; Story 3.2 is the gating validator).
   - Current `package.json` version is `0.10.0`; `npm version prerelease --preid=alpha` SHALL therefore produce `0.10.1-alpha.0` (NOT `1.0.0-alpha.0` — the v1.0.0 prerelease cycle is Story 5.2's territory, not 3.2's).

2. **Release environment allow-list temporarily widened to `feat/release-workflow`, captured for revoke.** Per `docs/RELEASING.md § Release Environment → Temporarily allowing a feature branch`, the dev agent SHALL:
   - `POST /repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/environments/release/deployment-branch-policies` with `{"name": "feat/release-workflow"}` and capture the returned `id` into a shell variable (`POLICY_ID`).
   - Confirm the POST returned a numeric `id` (not an error body) before proceeding — the command-substitution pattern in `docs/RELEASING.md` prevents the "eyeball-and-paste" footgun.
   - Treat the revoke (AC #8) as the final step of this story's execution, NOT a follow-up. A feature branch left on the allow-list is an unguarded hole per `docs/RELEASING.md:152-153`.

3. **Alpha cut dispatched against `feat/release-workflow`.** The `release.yaml` workflow SHALL be triggered with `version_bump: alpha` such that it runs against `feat/release-workflow` HEAD. Two candidate dispatch paths (decide at execution time per Task 3):
   - **Primary: `gh workflow run release.yaml --ref feat/release-workflow -f version_bump=alpha`** — uses the REST dispatch API against a non-default ref. This may or may not work depending on current GHA behavior (see Dev Notes § "Known unknown — workflow_dispatch from a non-default branch").
   - **Fallback: temporary `on: push: branches: [feat/release-workflow]` trigger added to `release.yaml` as a throwaway commit** — matches the Epic 1 Story 1.2 pattern (`epic-1-retro-2026-04-20.md § Patterns across all three stories → Task spec drifted from actual API/UI`, pivot to `on: push: branches: [throwaway/env-gate-test]`). If used, the trigger SHALL be reverted in a second commit on `feat/release-workflow` BEFORE Story 3.3 opens its merge PR (see AC #8).
   - Whichever path runs, the commit body / Dev Agent Record entry SHALL name the mechanism used and the run URL produced. Do NOT silently switch between them mid-run.

4. **Approval gate engages and is approved.** The workflow SHALL pause at the `environment: release` step for reviewer approval:
   - The Actions UI "Review deployments" prompt SHALL appear for the `release` environment.
   - The dev agent (or designated reviewer) SHALL approve via the UI; the approval event SHALL appear in the Actions run audit trail (NFR5 reconfirm).
   - The resume MUST NOT be automated — human click is the gate semantic (Story 1.2 invariant). `prevent_self_review: false` permits self-approval only as a solo-maintainer compatibility flag; the approval is recorded in the audit trail either way.

5. **Workflow steps complete clean, end-to-end.** Post-approval, every `release.yaml` step from Story 3.1 SHALL execute without error in order:
   - `Checkout` · `Setup Node.js` · `Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing` (`npm install -g npm@latest`) · `Install uv` · `Install dependencies` (`npm ci`) · `Run tests and validation` (`npm test` — full 12 subcommands).
   - `Configure Git` · `Bump version` (`npm version prerelease --preid=alpha`) → `0.10.1-alpha.0`.
   - `Get new version and previous tag` → outputs `new_version=0.10.1-alpha.0`, `previous_tag=v0.10.0`.
   - `Update marketplace.json version` → `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` `"version"` becomes `0.10.1-alpha.0`.
   - `Update CHANGELOG.md` → `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s` runs clean; a new entry is prepended **above** the `## [Unreleased]` anchor (or the anchor is consumed — Task 5 records which).
   - `Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG` → `grep -iE '(closes|fixes|resolves) \[#[^0-9]'` returns empty; step exits 0. (If the step trips, AC #11's defect protocol applies — this is the entire point of the guard.)
   - `Pre-publish dry-run` → `npm publish --dry-run` with `NPM_TOKEN: ""` passes.
   - `Commit version bump` · `Generate release notes` · `Create and push tag` → tag `v0.10.1-alpha.0` is pushed to `origin`.
   - `Push commit to main` is SKIPPED (`github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'` on a feature-branch dispatch — the Story 3.1 code-review patch D1 exists precisely for this path; the step SHALL fire `Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref)` instead, emitting `::notice::` with the expected message).
   - `Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing` → dist-tag chain selects `--tag alpha` (`VERSION == *"alpha"*`); `NPM_TOKEN: ""` is set; publish succeeds; NO `--provenance` flag passed (automatic under OIDC).
   - `Create GitHub Release` → `softprops/action-gh-release@v2` with `prerelease: true` (since version contains `alpha`).
   - `Summary` → `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` receives the expected block (header, npm URL, GitHub release URL, provenance line).

6. **npm-side verification — published, correctly tagged, provenance present.**
   - `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0` SHALL return the package manifest (non-404).
   - `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge dist-tags --json` SHALL show `"alpha": "0.10.1-alpha.0"` AND `"latest"` unchanged from its current value (the prior stable, `0.10.0`). The alpha version SHALL NOT appear under `"latest"` (FR3 invariant — the default-`latest` trap documented in research doc line 193).
   - **Provenance attestation evidence** — at least ONE of the following SHALL confirm a SLSA Build Level 2 provenance attestation is attached (NFR4; the Axios-March-2026 attack detection signal):
     - `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0 --json` output contains a non-null attestation reference (exact jq path varies by npm CLI version — `.dist.attestations`, top-level `.attestations`, or a `provenance`-keyed sub-object are all valid shapes; record the exact path that resolves).
     - `npm audit signatures --json` run against a fresh install of `bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0` reports a verified provenance signature.
     - The npm package page (`https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/0.10.1-alpha.0`) renders a "Provenance" badge with the expected GitHub Actions build metadata (repo, workflow, commit SHA).
   - At least one of the above methods SHALL be captured in Debug Log References verbatim (raw jq output, raw audit output, or screenshot URL) — prose "it worked" is insufficient.
   - `npm view bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0 --json | jq '.repository'` SHALL show the populated repository URL (required for provenance attestation to validate per research doc line 345).

7. **GitHub Release verification — created, prerelease-flagged, categorized notes.**
   - A GitHub Release tagged `v0.10.1-alpha.0` SHALL exist at `https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/releases/tag/v0.10.1-alpha.0`.
   - The release's `prerelease` flag SHALL be `true` (FR10 — set via the `contains(..., 'alpha')` expression in `release.yaml:177`).
   - The release body SHALL contain the categorized sections produced by Story 3.1's grep-categorization step (at minimum a `## What's New` header, an `## Installation` fenced code block, and a `**Full Changelog**:` compare URL of the form `https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/compare/v0.10.0...v0.10.1-alpha.0`).
   - The release title SHALL be `Skill Forge (SKF) v0.10.1-alpha.0`.

8. **Post-validation cleanup — allow-list revoked, any temp trigger reverted, tag/commit preserved.**
   - The `release` environment's `deployment-branch-policies` list SHALL be returned to `{main}` by calling `DELETE .../environments/release/deployment-branch-policies/<POLICY_ID>` with the id captured at AC #2. `gh api` GET of the policies SHALL subsequently return only `{name: "main"}`.
   - If AC #3 took the fallback path (temporary push trigger), the trigger SHALL be removed via a second commit on `feat/release-workflow` (`revert` or explicit edit reverting `on.push`), and the revert commit SHALL be pushed. `feat/release-workflow` HEAD SHALL end in a state where `release.yaml` has ONLY `on: workflow_dispatch` — this is the final shape Story 3.3's merge PR will carry to `main`.
   - **DO NOT force-push, reset, or delete the tag `v0.10.1-alpha.0`, the commit `release: bump to v0.10.1-alpha.0`, or the branch `feat/release-workflow` after the publish succeeds** (NFR12 reversibility boundary — once the alpha tag is pushed and the version is on npm, the release event is immutable; any attempt to "clean up" the git-side artifact creates an audit-trail inconsistency with the npm-side artifact that will remain forever).

9. **Artifact preservation invariant (NFR12 carry-over).** The published alpha version `0.10.1-alpha.0` is **permanently burned on npm** (the version number cannot be reused even after `npm unpublish` — see `docs/RELEASING.md § Rollback Playbook` Scenario C). It SHALL remain published. The git tag `v0.10.1-alpha.0` SHALL remain in `origin`. The GitHub Release `v0.10.1-alpha.0` SHALL remain (marked `prerelease`). Story 3.3, Story 5.x, and Story 6.x work with this as existing state.

10. **CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle — concrete behavior recorded.** Story 3.1 code-review D defer #3 (`deferred-work.md § "code review of 3-1..." → "## [Unreleased] anchor lifecycle after first release is unverified"`) made Story 3.2's alpha cut the live validator for preset append behavior on the seeded `CHANGELOG.md` with the hand-inserted `## [Unreleased]` anchor (Story 3.1 Task 4, CHANGELOG.md line 5). The dev agent SHALL:
    - Capture the exact CHANGELOG.md diff applied by the `Update CHANGELOG.md` step (e.g., via `git show v0.10.1-alpha.0:CHANGELOG.md | diff -u <pre-bump-CHANGELOG> -`).
    - Record whether the preset **preserved**, **replaced**, or **consumed** the `## [Unreleased]` heading; where the new `## [0.10.1-alpha.0]` entry landed relative to the anchor; and whether any re-insert step would be needed in `release.yaml` for the SECOND future release to remain well-formed.
    - If the observed behavior breaks the CHANGELOG shape (malformed heading, duplicate anchor, missing preamble), file the finding into Review Findings at AC #14 and flag it for Story 3.3 or Story 5.1 to patch.

11. **Defect-response protocol — every failure mode has a documented next step.** If any AC #4–#7 verification fails:
    - **Publish step 404 / "npm could not match your workflow run"**: follow the three-way comparison recipe in `docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher → Pre-registration inversion` — compare (a) npm Settings tab, (b) `release.yaml` `name` / `on` / `jobs.<id>.environment` lines, (c) the Registered table in RELEASING.md. **Do NOT re-dispatch until the specific mismatched field is identified.** Common culprit: `.yaml` vs `.yml` filename typo (Epic 1 carry-forward #1). Fix in a commit on `feat/release-workflow`, then AC #11-final applies.
    - **Approval gate never appears (workflow skipped / fired-and-forgot)**: the `environment: release` binding did not engage. Check (a) the dispatched ref is on the allow-list (AC #2), (b) `release.yaml:36` declares `environment: release`, (c) the env exists at all (`gh api .../environments/release`). Re-dispatch after fix; new alpha cut uses a FRESH version number (AC #11-final).
    - **Publish succeeds but tag/push failed**: follow `docs/RELEASING.md § Rollback Playbook Scenario E` once Story 4.1 lands it (Story 3.2 runs BEFORE Story 4.1; the playbook skeleton in research doc lines 963–971 is the interim reference). Manual `git tag -a v<version> <sha> -m ...` + `git push origin v<version>` + `gh release create ...`.
    - **Bogus-issue-ref assertion (AC #5's "Assert no bogus issue refs" step) trips**: this is the Story 2.2 class-of-bug regression caught in the wild. Inspect the offending CHANGELOG line, escalate to: (a) amend the offending historical commit body (only if the repo has zero downstream consumers of that tag — unlikely past v0.9.0), or (b) patch `release.yaml` with a post-generation `sed` to strip the specific non-numeric ref classes. Task is deferred-to-Story-3.2-dev-fix territory; do not ship the alpha with noisy refs.
    - **Any defect at all in `release.yaml`**: fix via a new commit on `feat/release-workflow` (NO force-push, NO amend of `34ad130`). The fresh alpha cut runs under a NEW alpha version (`npm version prerelease --preid=alpha` on `0.10.1-alpha.0` produces `0.10.1-alpha.1`). The burned `0.10.1-alpha.0` remains on npm with whatever state it was in — optionally `npm deprecate` it with a note once Story 4.1 ships the rollback playbook, or leave it unmarked since no downstream consumer is expected.

12. **Scope envelope — Story 3.2 does NOT modify source, skill, tool, or doc files.** The only files this story may change (and ONLY if AC #3's fallback path runs):
    - `.github/workflows/release.yaml` — a temporary `on: push: branches: [feat/release-workflow]` trigger addition + a revert commit. Both commits land on `feat/release-workflow` and ONLY `feat/release-workflow`. The final state of `release.yaml` on `feat/release-workflow` HEAD SHALL be identical to Story 3.1 close-out state (commit `34ad130`) modulo any intentional defect-fix commits from AC #11.

## Dev Notes

### What this story actually is

Story 3.2 is the **live validator**, not an authoring story. Story 3.1 authored `release.yaml` at `.github/workflows/release.yaml` on `feat/release-workflow` HEAD (`34ad130`, 187 lines after 5 code-review patches). Story 3.2's job is to run that file end-to-end against the real `npm` registry under real OIDC trusted publishing, and produce auditable evidence that the chain works.

**What Story 3.2 proves (that prose inspection cannot):**

1. **OIDC trusted-publisher handshake fires and succeeds.** The npm TP was registered 2026-04-20 (Story 1.3, pre-registration inversion) but has never been exercised. First live test is here.
2. **SLSA Build Level 2 provenance auto-attaches under OIDC.** No `--provenance` flag is passed; the attestation appears because `id-token: write` + registered TP. NFR4 ("100% of releases post-OIDC migration SHALL have an attached SLSA L2 attestation") is first operationalized here.
3. **The `environment: release` approval gate engages.** Story 1.2 proved env gate mechanics via a throwaway workflow; Story 3.2 proves the SAME gate engages on `release.yaml` specifically.
4. **Dist-tag routing works** — `alpha` version → `--tag alpha` (NOT `--tag latest`). The default-`latest` trap is the most-cited operational hazard in npm publishing (research doc line 193); Story 3.1's case-statement handles it, Story 3.2 verifies it.
5. **The `Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref)` path runs.** Story 3.1 code review patch D1 added this specifically for Story 3.2's feature-branch dispatch. First live exercise is here.
6. **CHANGELOG append behavior on the seeded file is concrete.** Story 3.1 deferred "CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle after first release is unverified" explicitly to Story 3.2's alpha cut (AC #10 / `deferred-work.md` 3-1 entry #3). This is the first time we learn what `conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s` actually does to the seeded file.
7. **Bogus-issue-ref regression guard is live.** Story 2.2's review caught 8 such refs by hand; Story 3.1 embedded the bash-assertion guard. Story 3.2 is the first post-guard release cut — if the guard trips, we catch the preset regressing in production; if it stays silent, the guard is proven viable.

**What Story 3.2 does NOT do** (to prevent scope creep):

- Does NOT merge `release.yaml` to `main` (Story 3.3's scope).
- Does NOT cut an RC or v1.0.0 (Story 5.2 + 5.3's scope — the version `0.10.1-alpha.0` produced here is explicitly a throwaway).
- Does NOT extend `docs/RELEASING.md` with rollback playbook content (Story 4.1's scope).
- Does NOT retire `publish.yaml` or `manual-release.yaml` — both remain in place as inert backup (Story 3.3 adds DEPRECATED headers + neutralizes `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag trigger; Story 6.1 deletes).
- Does NOT install or modify any build tooling or dependency.
- Does NOT modify `package.json` or `package-lock.json` (the workflow's CI run bumps `package.json` on an ephemeral runner; that mutation lives in the orphaned `release: bump` commit reachable only via the tag ref; `feat/release-workflow` HEAD is UNCHANGED).
- Does NOT cross-platform smoke test the published alpha (Story 5.2/5.4's scope — Story 3.2's optional `npm install bmad-module-skill-forge@alpha` is an opt-in belt-and-suspenders, not gating).

### Known unknown — `workflow_dispatch` from a non-default branch

**The constraint:** Per GitHub Actions docs and confirmed by Epic 1 Story 1.2 execution (`epic-1-retro § Patterns across all three stories → Task spec drifted from actual API/UI`), **`workflow_dispatch` fires ONLY if the workflow file exists on the default branch.** Story 3.2 dispatches against `feat/release-workflow`, and `release.yaml` does NOT exist on `main` yet (Story 3.3's scope).

**The primary attempt** (`gh workflow run release.yaml --ref feat/release-workflow -f version_bump=alpha`) may succeed because `gh` invokes the REST API directly, and GitHub's constraint has been loosened in some versions — but empirically we hit the strict interpretation in Story 1.2. The dev agent MUST try primary first; if it fails, fallback.

**The fallback:** replicate the Story 1.2 pattern — add a temporary `on: push: branches: [feat/release-workflow]` trigger to `release.yaml`. The next push to that branch fires the workflow automatically. Story 3.2's Task 6 reverts the trigger before Story 3.3 opens its merge PR so `release.yaml` on `main` has ONLY `workflow_dispatch`.

**Why not just merge `release.yaml` to `main` first?** Two reasons: (a) merging a workflow that has NEVER been dispatched is the scope Story 3.2 was created to gate — we would lose the "test before main" safety; (b) Story 3.3 AC #1 is explicit about Story 3.2 preceding the merge. The sequence epic design is load-bearing.

**Why not use a caller workflow (`workflow_call`)?** It's cleaner in the abstract but requires a deeper edit to `release.yaml` (adding `on: workflow_call`) which complicates Story 3.3's merge diff. The transient push-trigger + revert is surgical and matches the Epic 1 Story 1.2 precedent. Use it if primary fails.

**If BOTH paths fail unexpectedly:** STOP. Document the symptom in Dev Agent Record. Escalate — do NOT ad-hoc attempt a third mechanism (e.g., force-pushing a throwaway release.yaml to `main`) under pressure. The whole point of this story is to create auditable evidence, not artifacts of desperation.

### Release env allow-list — strict widen/revoke discipline

The `release` environment's `deployment_branch_policy` is `custom_branch_policies: true` with allow-list `{main}` only (Story 1.2 invariant). A `workflow_dispatch` (or push-trigger) against any other branch is rejected with the "branch not allowed" error — the `environment: release` binding engages before step-1 even runs.

Story 3.2 is the ONLY story in the current epic that legitimately needs to add a non-`main` entry to that list. The canonical pattern is `docs/RELEASING.md § Temporarily allowing a feature branch:132–153` (itself written with Story 3.2 in mind). The two-call shape is:

```bash
# Widen
POLICY_ID=$(gh api --method POST \
  repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/environments/release/deployment-branch-policies \
  --input - --jq .id <<'JSON'
{ "name": "feat/release-workflow" }
JSON
)

# ... run the validation cut, approve via UI, verify publish ...

# Revoke immediately
gh api --method DELETE \
  "repos/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/environments/release/deployment-branch-policies/$POLICY_ID"
```

**Failure modes & mitigations:**

- **`POST` returns `422 already_exists`** — the branch is already on the list (possibly from an aborted prior Story 3.2 attempt). Fetch the existing id via GET + jq and treat that as POLICY_ID for the revoke.
- **`POLICY_ID` is lost mid-session** (shell crash, network hiccup, context compaction) — Task 2 persists the id into `/tmp/release-env-policy-id.txt` as a backup. If even that's gone, fetch via GET and filter by name.
- **Revoke runs BEFORE the publish completes** (dev-agent ordering error) — the env gate fails on the resume; workflow aborts; no publish happens. Non-destructive; re-run the widen + dispatch.
- **Revoke is skipped entirely** — the allow-list has a trailing hole until the next maintainer notices. AC #8 treats this as a blocking defect; Story 3.2 is NOT complete until `deployment-branch-policies` returns to `{main}` only.

### Expected run shape — blow-by-blow

Based on Story 3.1 commit `34ad130`'s `release.yaml`, the alpha-cut run SHALL execute ~14 steps in this order (step names match `release.yaml` exactly):

| # | Step | Runtime (est.) | Expected output |
|---|------|---------------:|-----------------|
| 1 | Checkout | <10s | `feat/release-workflow` at `34ad130` (or defect-fix HEAD) checked out with `fetch-depth: 0` |
| 2 | Setup Node.js | <30s | Node 22.x (from `.nvmrc`) installed + npm cache primed |
| 3 | Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing | <20s | `npm install -g npm@latest` → `npm --version` ≥ 11.5.1 |
| 4 | Install uv | <15s | `uv` installed via `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6` (Python 3.12) |
| 5 | Install dependencies | <1m | `npm ci` — lockfile-driven install; `conventional-changelog-cli@5.0.0` resolves |
| 6 | Run tests and validation | ~2–4m | `npm test` — full 12 subcommands (prettier, eslint, markdownlint, validate:schemas, validate:refs, test:unit, test:agent-bmad, test:agent-brainstorming, test:integration, test:skill-rules, test:python). Matches `quality.yaml` matrix logic |
| 7 | **Pause at `environment: release` gate** | ≤5m (human wait) | "Review deployments" prompt in UI; approver clicks → resume |
| 8 | Configure Git | <1s | `github-actions[bot]` identity set |
| 9 | Bump version | <5s | `npm version prerelease --preid=alpha` on `0.10.0` → `0.10.1-alpha.0`; `package.json` + `package-lock.json` updated in-runner |
| 10 | Get new version and previous tag | <1s | outputs: `new_version=0.10.1-alpha.0`, `previous_tag=v0.10.0` |
| 11 | Update marketplace.json version | <1s | `.claude-plugin/marketplace.json` `"version": "0.10.1-alpha.0"` |
| 12 | Update CHANGELOG.md | <5s | `npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s` — **append behavior is the AC #10 observation target** |
| 13 | Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG | <1s | `grep -iE '(closes\|fixes\|resolves) \[#[^0-9]'` empty → exit 0 |
| 14 | Pre-publish dry-run | ~10s | `npm publish --dry-run` with `NPM_TOKEN: ""` — tarball manifest printed; exits 0 |
| 15 | Commit version bump | <1s | ephemeral commit `release: bump to v0.10.1-alpha.0` on runner HEAD |
| 16 | Generate release notes | <2s | `git log v0.10.0..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse` categorized into `### New Features` / `### Bug Fixes` / `### Other Changes` / `### Maintenance` |
| 17 | Create and push tag | ~5s | `git tag -a v0.10.1-alpha.0 -m ...` + `git push origin v0.10.1-alpha.0` → tag visible in GitHub tags list (pointing to the ephemeral bump commit) |
| 18 | Push commit to main | SKIPPED | `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` false on feature-branch dispatch |
| 19 | Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref) | <1s | `::notice::Dispatched from refs/heads/feat/release-workflow ...` — proof the Story 3.1 patch D1 path is live |
| 20 | Publish to npm via OIDC | ~15–30s | `npm publish --tag alpha` (OIDC handshake, no `NPM_TOKEN`, no `--provenance` flag) — success: provenance auto-attached |
| 21 | Create GitHub Release | ~5s | `softprops/action-gh-release@v2` creates `v0.10.1-alpha.0` release with `prerelease: true` |
| 22 | Summary | <1s | `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` block — header, npm URL, GitHub URL, provenance line |

**Total wall-clock estimate:** 5–10 min CI runtime + human-approval wait (NFR1 target: <15 min CI + <5 min approval).

### Load-bearing decisions — resolved at story-level

**(D1) Dispatch mechanism — primary vs fallback.** Task 3 runs the primary `gh workflow run --ref` first. Fallback only if primary fails. Decision point at execution time, NOT at story-authoring time, because GHA's `workflow_dispatch` constraint behavior has shifted across GitHub's release cycle and the dev agent's live probe is the ground truth.

**(D2) POLICY_ID persistence path.** Shell-var-only is brittle across session boundaries (compaction, network, manual context shifts). Task 2 writes the id to `/tmp/release-env-policy-id.txt` as a durable backup before the run. Task 6 reads either source; either works.

**(D3) Defect-fix commits stay on `feat/release-workflow`, NOT on `main` or a new branch.** Any `release.yaml` fixes Story 3.2 needs to ship to validate are simply new commits on `feat/release-workflow`. Story 3.3's merge PR will carry them to `main` along with the Story 3.1 baseline. This keeps the branch-topology clean and the merge audit trail single-stranded.

**(D4) Do NOT `npm deprecate` the test alpha unless a real problem is found.** The version `0.10.1-alpha.0` is a throwaway — even if it ships with minor cosmetics (e.g., a weird CHANGELOG shape from the first append-mode run), it is not a consumer-facing version (no one will `npm install bmad-module-skill-forge@alpha` intentionally yet, and `latest` still points at `0.10.0`). `npm deprecate` on this version is reserved for genuine defects uncovered in Task 5 (e.g., broken tarball, provenance absent, wrong dist-tag). Low-effort, reversible decision — take it at Task 7 close-out.

7. **Evidence completeness** — Dev Agent Record § Debug Log References populated per AC #13.

Each of these is captured in the Dev Agent Record as a falsifiable claim with a specific command or URL — not a prose assertion.

## Project Structure Notes

**Files Story 3.2 may modify (and the rationale):**

- `.github/workflows/release.yaml` — ONLY if Task 3 fallback runs (temporary push trigger + revert) OR AC #11 defect fix requires a patch. End-state SHALL be byte-identical to Story 3.1 close-out (`34ad130`) modulo intentional fixes. If no fallback and no defect, ZERO file edits.
- Untracked / ephemeral: `/tmp/release-env-policy-id.txt`, `/tmp/changelog-pre-alpha-cut.md`, `/tmp/changelog-post-alpha-cut.md`. Not committed.
- This story's own artifact: `_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-2-validate-release-yaml-via-alpha-test-cut-from-feature-branch.md` (this file, + Dev Agent Record populated at execution time).

**Files Story 3.2 MUST NOT modify:**

- `package.json` / `package-lock.json` — the workflow mutates these in-runner as part of the bump, but that mutation lives in the orphaned `release: bump` commit reachable only via the alpha tag. `feat/release-workflow` HEAD's `package.json` version stays `0.10.0` throughout Story 3.2.
- `CHANGELOG.md` — same story: the workflow's append lands in the orphaned commit; the branch HEAD's file is unchanged. AC #10's lifecycle observation works by diffing the orphaned-commit file against the pre-cut branch file.
- `.github/workflows/publish.yaml` / `.github/workflows/manual-release.yaml` — Story 3.3 + 6.1 territory.
- `.github/workflows/quality.yaml` — Story 3.1 already added the Windows smoke step. Story 3.2 does not touch.
- `docs/RELEASING.md` — Story 3.2 USES the § Release Environment + § npm Trusted Publisher sections as reference; does not modify them. Story 4.1 owns the § Rollback Playbook expansion.
- `docs/STABILITY.md` — Story 5.1 territory.
- `README.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `src/**`, `test/**`, `tools/**` — orthogonal.

**Repo-level state changes Story 3.2 makes (outside of files):**

- Temporarily adds `feat/release-workflow` to `release` env `deployment_branch_policies`, then removes (Task 2 + Task 6).
- Pushes the tag `v0.10.1-alpha.0` to `origin` (via the CI workflow's `git push origin v0.10.1-alpha.0` step).
- Publishes `bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0` to `registry.npmjs.org` under `--tag alpha` (via the CI workflow's `npm publish` step). **PERMANENT** — npm versions are immutable; the version number is burned even after `npm unpublish`.
- Creates a GitHub Release `v0.10.1-alpha.0` (via softprops action), marked `prerelease: true`.
- Produces a GitHub Actions run record (audit trail for NFR5 / NFR10).
- Produces an OIDC provenance attestation record on `npmjs.com`, verifiable via `npm view ... --json | jq '.dist.attestations'`.

### References

- [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md § Story-3.2-Validate-release-yaml-via-alpha-test-cut-from-feature-branch`] — AC source (5 bracketed clusters). This file consolidates into ACs 1–14 with coverage preserved.
- [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md § Epic-3-Consolidated-OIDC-Release-Workflow`] — Epic objectives; FR coverage map. Story 3.2 does NOT own any FR directly (it's a validation story); NFR1 (cycle time) + NFR2 (failure rate) + NFR4 (provenance) are first operationalized here.
- [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/research/technical-skf-release-workflow-research-2026-04-19.md § Migration-Steps:893-897`] — Migration Step 4: "Add new workflow alongside existing" — steps 2–3 ("Test by running `workflow_dispatch` with `version_bump: alpha`, target a temp branch. Verify: OIDC publish succeeds, provenance attestation appears on npm, GitHub release is created."). Story 3.2 is this step made concrete.
- [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/research/technical-skf-release-workflow-research-2026-04-19.md § Dist-Tag-Semantics:191-196`] — dist-tag default-`latest` trap; Story 3.2 AC #6 verifies the trap was avoided.
- [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/research/technical-skf-release-workflow-research-2026-04-19.md § Identity-Signing-Provenance-Layer:141-160`] — OIDC trusted publishing mechanics; the March-2026 Axios attack as the motivating example for NFR4 provenance enforcement.
- [Source: `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/research/technical-skf-release-workflow-research-2026-04-19.md § OIDC-Trusted-Publisher-Integration-Mechanics:321-351`] — TP binding on org+repo+workflow-filename+env; `.yaml` vs `.yml` gotcha; `--provenance` flag becomes a no-op.

All load-bearing for Story 3.2's verification.

content.text:
Found next ready story: **3-2-validate-release-yaml-via-alpha-test-cut-from-feature-branch**. Before I start executing, I need to flag the scope — this is a live validation story with irreversible side effects on shared infra, not a code-change story.

**What this story actually does:**
1. **Modifies `release` GitHub Environment** — temporarily adds `feat/release-workflow` to the deployment-branch allow-list (reversible at Task 6).
2. **Publishes `bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0` to npm permanently** — per AC #9, the version number is burned forever even after `npm unpublish`. Irreversible.
3. **Pushes tag `v0.10.1-alpha.0`** + creates a public GitHub Release (`prerelease: true`).
4. **Requires YOU to click "Review deployments → Approve"** in the Actions UI — the `environment: release` gate is a human-in-the-loop block I literally cannot bypass.
5. **Requires YOU to visually check** the npm Trusted Publisher UI (Task 1) and the provenance badge (Task 5).

Near-zero file changes in the repo itself (only if fallback dispatch path runs or defects force a patch on `release.yaml`).

**Before I touch anything remote, confirm:**

1. **Proceed with the live alpha cut against npm?** This is the first live exercise of the OIDC Trusted Publisher registered 2026-04-20. Story 3.3 is blocked on it. If you say no, the story stays in `ready-for-dev`.
2. **You'll be present for the approval click** when the workflow pauses? Without you clicking Approve in the GH Actions UI, the run sits forever and times out.
3. **OK with me executing Task 1 (read-only recon via `gh api` / `npm view` / `git` probes) right now** to confirm baseline state is intact, independent of (1)?

Reply **"proceed"** to run all of it end-to-end (I'll pause at the approval-click moment and wait for you), **"recon only"** to just run Task 1 and report back before deciding, or **"hold"** to stop.

#   - backlog: Epic not yet started
#   - in-progress: Epic actively being worked on
#   - done: All stories in epic completed
#
# Epic Status Transitions:
#   - backlog → in-progress: Automatically when first story is created (via create-story)
#   - in-progress → done: Manually when all stories reach 'done' status
#
# Story Status:
#   - backlog: Story only exists in epic file
#   - ready-for-dev: Story file created in stories folder
#   - in-progress: Developer actively working on implementation
#   - review: Ready for code review (via Dev's code-review workflow)
#   - done: Story completed
#
# Retrospective Status:
#   - optional: Can be completed but not required
#   - done: Retrospective has been completed
#
# WORKFLOW NOTES:
# ===============
# - Epic transitions to 'in-progress' automatically when first story is created
# - Stories can be worked in parallel if team capacity allows
# - Developer typically creates next story after previous one is 'done' to incorporate learnings
# - Dev moves story to 'review', then runs code-review (fresh context, different LLM recommended)

content.content:
name: Quality & Validation

# Runs comprehensive quality checks on all PRs:
# - Prettier (formatting)
# - ESLint (linting)
# - markdownlint (markdown quality)
# - Schema validation (YAML structure)
# - Agent schema tests (fixture-based validation)
# - Installation component tests (CLI + compilation)
# - Workflow state consistency tests
# - Knowledge base tests
# - Skill structure validation
# - File reference validation
# - Python script tests (pytest via uv)

"on":
  pull_request:
    branches: ["**"]
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  prettier:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
===
name: Health-Check Dedup

# Server-side dedup for workflow health-check issues.
# When a new issue carrying the `health-check` label is opened, this Action:
#   1. Extracts the fingerprint from any `fp-XXXXXXX` label on the issue.
#   2. Searches for an earlier OPEN issue with the same fingerprint.
#   3. If one exists (and has a lower issue number — tiebreak for races):
#      - Posts a short "duplicate of #N" comment on the new issue.
#      - Adds the `duplicate` label and closes the new issue.
#      - Posts a 👍 reaction on the canonical issue to preserve the signal-count.
#   4. If no prior open issue exists, does nothing — the new issue becomes canonical.
#
# This is a safety net. The client-side dedup in src/shared/health-check.md
# handles the common case; this Action catches races and manual-filing cases.

"on":
  issues:
    types: [opened, labeled]

permissions:
  issues: write

jobs:
  dedup:
    if: contains(github.event.issue.labels.*.name, 'health-check')

permissions:

jobs:

    if: contains(github.event.issue.labels.*.name, 'health-check')

content.content:
15:1. **File greenfield-created** at `.github/workflows/release.yaml` on the current `feat/release-workflow` branch. The filename MUST be literal `release.yaml` (NOT `.yml`) — the npm Trusted Publisher registered 2026-04-20 binds character-for-character on `release.yaml` (see Dev Notes § "Epic 1 retro carry-forward #1 — `.yaml` vs `.yml` enforcement"). Reviewer SHALL verify filename at code-review time.
22:3. **Environment gate** (FR11): the publishing job SHALL declare `environment: release` so GitHub pauses for a required-reviewer approval before any mutation step (bump / commit / tag / push / publish). Gate references the env from Story 1.2.
43:[Omitted long matching line]
45:9. **Commit + tag + push** (FR9):
50:   - Push tag (`git push origin v${{ ... }}`) and push HEAD commit to `main` (`git push origin HEAD:main`).
72:[Omitted long matching line]
74:15. **Do NOT merge to main in this story.** The workflow MUST be created on `feat/release-workflow` only. Story 3.2 validates via alpha cut from the feature branch; Story 3.3 owns the main-merge plus atomic `publish.yaml` tag-trigger neutralization (Epic 1 retro carry-forward #2).
84:18. **Full quality suite passes on `feat/release-workflow` HEAD after commit.** `npm test` SHALL remain green (the only file-level additions are `.github/workflows/*` — outside the Prettier + ESLint + markdownlint surface for `**/*.{js,cjs,mjs,json,yaml}` formatted via `prettier --check`). YAML schema validation (`npm run validate:schemas`) SHALL pass on the new workflow file. Reviewer SHALL verify no pre-existing green check drifted to red as a side effect.
175:The file is authored **on `feat/release-workflow` only**. Do NOT merge to `main` — that is Story 3.3's scope and is coupled with an atomic neutralization of `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag trigger (Epic 1 retro carry-forward #2 / `deferred-work.md § "Story 3.3 precondition"`). Merging Story 3.1 alone leaves a window where both workflows can fire on the same tag push.
196:- `publish.yaml` — **present** (40 lines; `on: push: tags: v*`; uses `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}` and `npm publish --provenance`; to be retained inert per Story 3.3, its tag trigger neutralized in the same PR)
209:- Branch protection on `main`: ruleset `13855503`, 7 required status checks (`quality.yaml` matrix jobs), required PR reviews, no force-push, `prevent_self_review: false` (standing flag).
233:**Story 3.3 PR atomically neutralizes `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag trigger.** This is Story 3.3's territory, not 3.1's. `publish.yaml` stays UNTOUCHED in Story 3.1. The reason the coupling is atomic in 3.3 and not 3.1: until `release.yaml` is merged to main, `publish.yaml` is the active fallback if a contributor pushes a `v*` tag by accident. Neutralizing its trigger in 3.1 (while `release.yaml` is still on a feature branch) would break the fallback path without having replaced it.
282:- HEAD commit push to `main` — lines 141-148 (but simplify: `release.yaml`'s main push is load-bearing per research doc line 822, not optional).
449:- `main`-push step simplified per research doc line 822 (`git push origin HEAD:main` with no fallback); manual-release's graceful-fail branch (lines 141-148) is deliberately dropped — the research doc calls this push "load-bearing, not optional."
476:- **Story 3.2** — consumer of this workflow. The authored `release.yaml` expects to be triggered via `workflow_dispatch` with `version_bump: alpha` on `feat/release-workflow` HEAD. Story 3.2's first alpha cut is the live validator for the full OIDC chain (publish → provenance → dist-tag routing → GitHub Release). If that cut 404s, the three-way comparison recipe in `docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher` is the triage path.
477:- **Story 3.3** — consumer that owns the `main` merge. This story's author deliberately left the workflow on `feat/release-workflow` only. Story 3.3's PR MUST atomically neutralize `publish.yaml`'s `v*` tag trigger per Epic 1 retro carry-forward #2 (`deferred-work.md § "Story 3.3 precondition"`), else there is a window where both workflows fire on the same tag push.
498:- [x] [Review][Patch] **Gate `git push origin HEAD:main` on `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'`** [release.yaml:143-150] — APPLIED. Added `if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` to the "Push commit to main" step plus a sibling "Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref)" step that logs an explanatory `::notice::` for non-main dispatches. Story 3.2's alpha cut from `feat/release-workflow` will now cleanly skip the main push (tag is still pushed; main is correctly NOT advanced). (Originally D1.)
499:[Omitted long matching line]
506:[Omitted long matching line]

content.text:
**Task 1 recon — all green, one precondition gap flagged:**

| Probe | Result |
|---|---|
| HEAD | `34ad1301c4d8cf11d281cafcc6127ceecc16c181` (matches `34ad130`) ✓ |
| main | `ef22801`; no `release.yaml` present ✓ |
| `release.yaml` filename | literal `.yaml`, UTF-8, 187 lines ✓ |
| release env | id `14347249917`, reviewer `armelhbobdad`, `prevent_self_review: false`, `custom_branch_policies: true` ✓ |
| deployment-branch-policies | `[{id:47435919, name:"main"}]` only (baseline for AC #8 revert) ✓ |
| release env secrets | `0` ✓ |
| `package.json` version | `"0.10.0"` ✓ |
| `package.json.repository.url` | populated (provenance precondition) ✓ |
| `.nvmrc` | `22` ✓ |
| `conventional-changelog-cli` devDep | `^5.0.0` ✓ |
| npm dist-tags | `{latest: 0.10.0}` only (no `alpha` tag yet) ✓ |
| `npm view @0.10.1-alpha.0` | 404 ✓ (clean version slot) |
| CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` anchor | line 5, snapshotted `/tmp/changelog-pre-alpha-cut.md` (259 lines) ✓ |
| existing `v0.10.1-alpha*` tag | none ✓ |

**Gap:** `feat/release-workflow` is committed locally at `34ad130` but **never pushed to origin** (origin has only `dev`, `entire/checkpoints/v1`, `feat/windows-support`, `main`). Story 3.1 left it local-only. Dispatch requires the remote ref.

Pushing is safe — no CI fires: `quality.yaml` is PR-only, `release.yaml`/`manual-release.yaml` are `workflow_dispatch`-only, `publish.yaml` is tag-only, `docs.yaml` is push-to-main only. Only side effect is a Discord `create` notification (cosmetic).

Also: I can't probe the **npm Trusted Publisher UI** (four fields verbatim) — that's browser-only per AC #1 / `docs/RELEASING.md`. You'll need to spot-check that yourself before we approve at the gate. Do it now if you haven't recently.

Pushing `feat/release-workflow` now, then proceeding to Task 2.

file.content:
name: Release

# Canonical OIDC trusted-publishing release workflow for bmad-module-skill-forge.
# Consolidates publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml. Gated on the `release` GitHub
# Environment (required-reviewer approval). Filename MUST remain literal
# `release.yaml` — the npm Trusted Publisher is registered character-for-character
# on `release.yaml` + job id `release`. See docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version_bump:
        description: Version bump type
        required: true
        default: alpha
        type: choice
        options:
          - alpha
          - beta
          - rc
          - patch
          - minor
          - major

permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: release
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: release
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm
          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org

      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: npm install -g npm@latest

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests and validation
        run: npm test

      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          case "${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} ;;
            *)             npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
        run: |
          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s

      - name: Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG
        run: |
          if grep -iE '(closes|fixes|resolves) \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md; then
            echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md contains non-numeric '(closes|fixes|resolves) [#...]' refs — aborting release."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: npm publish --dry-run

      - name: Commit version bump
        run: |
          git add package.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json CHANGELOG.md
          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: release_notes
        run: |
          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)
          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)
          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)

          {
            echo "## What's New"
            echo
            [ -n "$FEATURES" ] && { echo "### New Features"; echo "$FEATURES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$FIXES" ]    && { echo "### Bug Fixes"; echo "$FIXES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$OTHERS" ]   && { echo "### Other Changes"; echo "$OTHERS"; echo; }
            [ -n "$CHORES" ]   && { echo "### Maintenance"; echo "$CHORES"; echo; }
            echo "## Installation"
            echo
            echo '```bash'
            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
            echo '```'
            echo
            echo "**Full Changelog**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          } > release_notes.md

          {
            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
            cat release_notes.md
            echo "EOF"
          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create and push tag
        run: |
          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
          else
            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          fi

      - name: Push commit to main
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: git push origin HEAD:main

      - name: Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref)
        if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          echo "::notice::Dispatched from ${{ github.ref }} (not main); skipping 'git push origin HEAD:main'."
          echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} was pushed; main was NOT advanced."
          echo "This is the expected path for prerelease cuts from feature branches (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern)."

      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
          fi

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          {
            echo "## 🎉 Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo
            echo "**npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**GitHub**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Release

# Canonical OIDC trusted-publishing release workflow for bmad-module-skill-forge.
# Consolidates publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml. Gated on the `release` GitHub
# Environment (required-reviewer approval). Filename MUST remain literal
# `release.yaml` — the npm Trusted Publisher is registered character-for-character
# on `release.yaml` + job id `release`. See docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version_bump:
        description: Version bump type
        required: true
        default: alpha
        type: choice
        options:
          - alpha
          - beta
          - rc
          - patch
          - minor
          - major
  push:
    branches: [feat/release-workflow]

permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: release
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: release
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm
          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org

      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: npm install -g npm@latest

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests and validation
        run: npm test

      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          case "${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} ;;
            *)             npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
        run: |
          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s

      - name: Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG
        run: |
          if grep -iE '(closes|fixes|resolves) \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md; then
            echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md contains non-numeric '(closes|fixes|resolves) [#...]' refs — aborting release."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: npm publish --dry-run

      - name: Commit version bump
        run: |
          git add package.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json CHANGELOG.md
          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: release_notes
        run: |
          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)
          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)
          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)

          {
            echo "## What's New"
            echo
            [ -n "$FEATURES" ] && { echo "### New Features"; echo "$FEATURES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$FIXES" ]    && { echo "### Bug Fixes"; echo "$FIXES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$OTHERS" ]   && { echo "### Other Changes"; echo "$OTHERS"; echo; }
            [ -n "$CHORES" ]   && { echo "### Maintenance"; echo "$CHORES"; echo; }
            echo "## Installation"
            echo
            echo '```bash'
            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
            echo '```'
            echo
            echo "**Full Changelog**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          } > release_notes.md

          {
            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
            cat release_notes.md
            echo "EOF"
          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create and push tag
        run: |
          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
          else
            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          fi

      - name: Push commit to main
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: git push origin HEAD:main

      - name: Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref)
        if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          echo "::notice::Dispatched from ${{ github.ref }} (not main); skipping 'git push origin HEAD:main'."
          echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} was pushed; main was NOT advanced."
          echo "This is the expected path for prerelease cuts from feature branches (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern)."

      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
          fi

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          {
            echo "## 🎉 Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo
            echo "**npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**GitHub**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

input.command:
git add .github/workflows/release.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(release): add temporary push trigger for Story 3.2 alpha cut (revert in same story)

Adds `on: push: branches: [feat/release-workflow]` + defaults the `Bump version`
step to `alpha` when `github.event.inputs.version_bump` is empty (push event has
no inputs). Needed because `workflow_dispatch` requires the workflow to exist on
the default branch, but `release.yaml` is only on feat/release-workflow until
Story 3.3 merges it to main.

This commit SHALL be reverted in Story 3.2 Task 6 before Story 3.3 opens its
merge PR. Final state of release.yaml on feat/release-workflow HEAD MUST be
byte-identical to 34ad130 (Story 3.1 close-out).

Refs: Story 3.2 Task 3 fallback path; epic-1-retro-2026-04-20 § "Task spec
drifted from actual API/UI" precedent.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -20

content.text:
Real defect found — `npm install -g npm@latest` fails with `Cannot find module 'promise-retry'`, a known npm self-upgrade race where the bundled arborist's `rebuild.js` runs mid-upgrade while the `node_modules/` tree is in a partial state. Failure was pre-bump, so **`0.10.1-alpha.0` slot is still free** (nothing published, no tag).

Applying defect-fix per AC #11. Adding `--force` to work around the rebuild race.

input.old_string:
- name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: npm install -g npm@latest
input.new_string:
- name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: |
          echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
          npm install -g --force npm@latest
          echo "Upgraded npm: $(npm --version)"
