toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Release

# Canonical OIDC trusted-publishing release workflow for bmad-module-skill-forge.
# Consolidates publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml. Gated on the `release` GitHub
# Environment (required-reviewer approval). Filename MUST remain literal
# `release.yaml` — the npm Trusted Publisher is registered character-for-character
# on `release.yaml` + job id `release`. See docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version_bump:
        description: Version bump type
        required: true
        default: alpha
        type: choice
        options:
          - alpha
          - beta
          - rc
          - patch
          - minor
          - major
  push:
    branches: [feat/release-workflow]

permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: release
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: release
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm
          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org

      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: npm install -g npm@latest

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests and validation
        run: npm test

      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          BUMP="${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}"
          BUMP="${BUMP:-alpha}"
          case "$BUMP" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid="$BUMP" ;;
            *)             npm version "$BUMP" --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
        run: |
          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s

      - name: Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG
        run: |
          if grep -iE '(closes|fixes|resolves) \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md; then
            echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md contains non-numeric '(closes|fixes|resolves) [#...]' refs — aborting release."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: npm publish --dry-run

      - name: Commit version bump
        run: |
          git add package.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json CHANGELOG.md
          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: release_notes
        run: |
          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)
          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)
          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)

          {
            echo "## What's New"
            echo
            [ -n "$FEATURES" ] && { echo "### New Features"; echo "$FEATURES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$FIXES" ]    && { echo "### Bug Fixes"; echo "$FIXES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$OTHERS" ]   && { echo "### Other Changes"; echo "$OTHERS"; echo; }
            [ -n "$CHORES" ]   && { echo "### Maintenance"; echo "$CHORES"; echo; }
            echo "## Installation"
            echo
            echo '```bash'
            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
            echo '```'
            echo
            echo "**Full Changelog**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          } > release_notes.md

          {
            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
            cat release_notes.md
            echo "EOF"
          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create and push tag
        run: |
          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
          else
            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          fi

      - name: Push commit to main
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: git push origin HEAD:main

      - name: Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref)
        if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          echo "::notice::Dispatched from ${{ github.ref }} (not main); skipping 'git push origin HEAD:main'."
          echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} was pushed; main was NOT advanced."
          echo "This is the expected path for prerelease cuts from feature branches (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern)."

      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
          fi

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          {
            echo "## 🎉 Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo
            echo "**npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**GitHub**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

input.command:
git add .github/workflows/release.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(release): force npm self-upgrade to survive arborist rebuild race

`npm install -g npm@latest` on GitHub-hosted Node 22.22.2 runners fails with
`Cannot find module 'promise-retry'` during arborist rebuild — classic self-
upgrade race where the bundled npm's @npmcli/arborist/lib/arborist/rebuild.js
runs after node_modules/ has already been partially overwritten by the new
npm. `--force` skips the conflict check, letting the install complete.

Also adds diagnostic echos (before/after npm version) so future failures of
this step surface the relevant state in the log without extra debugging.

Caught by Story 3.2 alpha-cut run 24714037093 (push event; failed at step 4
pre-bump — `0.10.1-alpha.0` slot not burned). AC #11 defect-protocol path.

Refs: Story 3.2 Task 3 defect-fix; release run
https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24714037093
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -15

content.text: `--force` doesn't fix it — the bundled npm is `10.9.7` and the self-upgrade corrupts its own `node_modules/` tree mid-install. Switching to a side-prefix install (new npm in `$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp/`, prepended to PATH via `$GITHUB_PATH`) — avoids touching the old npm's tree entirely.

input.old_string:
- name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: |
          echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
          npm install -g --force npm@latest
          echo "Upgraded npm: $(npm --version)"
input.new_string:
- name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: |
          echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
          # Side-prefix install — avoids the arborist rebuild race that bites
          # `npm install -g npm@latest` on Node 22.22.2's bundled npm 10.9.7
          # (old arborist cannot find its own promise-retry after the global
          # tree has been partially overwritten by the new npm mid-install).
          INSTALL_DIR="$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp"
          mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"
          npm install --prefix "$INSTALL_DIR" --no-save npm@latest
          echo "$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
          echo "Upgraded npm: $("$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin/npm" --version)"

content.content: The file /home/armel/Projects/OSS/bmad-module-skill-forge/.github/workflows/release.yaml has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

toolUseResult.oldString:
- name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: |
          echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
          npm install -g --force npm@latest
          echo "Upgraded npm: $(npm --version)"
toolUseResult.newString:
- name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: |
          echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
          # Side-prefix install — avoids the arborist rebuild race that bites
          # `npm install -g npm@latest` on Node 22.22.2's bundled npm 10.9.7
          # (old arborist cannot find its own promise-retry after the global
          # tree has been partially overwritten by the new npm mid-install).
          INSTALL_DIR="$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp"
          mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"
          npm install --prefix "$INSTALL_DIR" --no-save npm@latest
          echo "$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
          echo "Upgraded npm: $("$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin/npm" --version)"
toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Release

# Canonical OIDC trusted-publishing release workflow for bmad-module-skill-forge.
# Consolidates publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml. Gated on the `release` GitHub
# Environment (required-reviewer approval). Filename MUST remain literal
# `release.yaml` — the npm Trusted Publisher is registered character-for-character
# on `release.yaml` + job id `release`. See docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version_bump:
        description: Version bump type
        required: true
        default: alpha
        type: choice
        options:
          - alpha
          - beta
          - rc
          - patch
          - minor
          - major
  push:
    branches: [feat/release-workflow]

permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: release
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: release
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm
          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org

      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: |
          echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
          npm install -g --force npm@latest
          echo "Upgraded npm: $(npm --version)"

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests and validation
        run: npm test

      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          BUMP="${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}"
          BUMP="${BUMP:-alpha}"
          case "$BUMP" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid="$BUMP" ;;
            *)             npm version "$BUMP" --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
        run: |
          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s

      - name: Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG
        run: |
          if grep -iE '(closes|fixes|resolves) \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md; then
            echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md contains non-numeric '(closes|fixes|resolves) [#...]' refs — aborting release."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: npm publish --dry-run

      - name: Commit version bump
        run: |
          git add package.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json CHANGELOG.md
          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: release_notes
        run: |
          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)
          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)
          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)

          {
            echo "## What's New"
            echo
            [ -n "$FEATURES" ] && { echo "### New Features"; echo "$FEATURES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$FIXES" ]    && { echo "### Bug Fixes"; echo "$FIXES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$OTHERS" ]   && { echo "### Other Changes"; echo "$OTHERS"; echo; }
            [ -n "$CHORES" ]   && { echo "### Maintenance"; echo "$CHORES"; echo; }
            echo "## Installation"
            echo
            echo '```bash'
            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
            echo '```'
            echo
            echo "**Full Changelog**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          } > release_notes.md

          {
            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
            cat release_notes.md
            echo "EOF"
          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create and push tag
        run: |
          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
          else
            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          fi

      - name: Push commit to main
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: git push origin HEAD:main

      - name: Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref)
        if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          echo "::notice::Dispatched from ${{ github.ref }} (not main); skipping 'git push origin HEAD:main'."
          echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} was pushed; main was NOT advanced."
          echo "This is the expected path for prerelease cuts from feature branches (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern)."

      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
          fi

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          {
            echo "## 🎉 Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo
            echo "**npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**GitHub**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

lines: - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
run: |
echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
-          npm install -g --force npm@latest
-          echo "Upgraded npm: $(npm --version)"
+          # Side-prefix install — avoids the arborist rebuild race that bites
+          # `npm install -g npm@latest` on Node 22.22.2's bundled npm 10.9.7
+          # (old arborist cannot find its own promise-retry after the global
+          # tree has been partially overwritten by the new npm mid-install).
+          INSTALL_DIR="$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp"
+          mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"
+          npm install --prefix "$INSTALL_DIR" --no-save npm@latest
+          echo "$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
+          echo "Upgraded npm: $("$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin/npm" --version)"
- name: Install uv
uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6

input.command:
git add .github/workflows/release.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(release): side-prefix npm install to dodge self-upgrade rebuild race

Prior `npm install -g --force npm@latest` still crashes on Node 22.22.2's
bundled npm 10.9.7: the global install partially overwrites /opt/hostedtool-
cache/.../lib/node_modules/npm/ before the arborist rebuild step runs, so
rebuild.js's require('promise-retry') fails — the old arborist is looking
for its own dep in a tree the new npm has already started replacing. `--force`
doesn't help because the issue isn't conflict resolution; it's that the tree
is in a partial state at the moment arborist tries to read from it.

Fix: install `npm@latest` to $RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp (a side prefix) and prepend
$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin to $GITHUB_PATH so subsequent steps pick it up.
The old npm's /opt/hostedtoolcache/.../lib/node_modules/npm/ tree stays
unchanged, which means arborist's rebuild step resolves promise-retry from
its own intact node_modules. The new npm runs from a different location and
its dep tree is self-contained.

Ran diagnostic echo confirms bundled npm is 10.9.7 — below the 11.5.1 floor
the trusted-publisher step requires, so the upgrade is load-bearing.

Refs: Story 3.2 Task 3 defect-fix cascade; second failed alpha-cut run
https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24714258553
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

content.subject: Task 5 — Post-publish verification (npm + GH Release + CHANGELOG)
content.description: npm view dist-tags, provenance jq verification, gh release view, CHANGELOG [Unreleased] lifecycle diff + three-question answers

content.subject: Task 3 — Decide dispatch mechanism and execute
content.description: Try primary `gh workflow run release.yaml --ref feat/release-workflow -f version_bump=alpha`; if fails, fallback to temporary on:push trigger commit + revert

content.subject: Task 7 — Record evidence and close story
content.description: Populate Dev Agent Record Debug Log References + Completion Notes + File List + Change Log; flip Status to review; update sprint-status.yaml

content.subject: Task 4 — Approve at gate and watch run to completion
content.description: Watch run; pause for user's manual UI approval at environment: release gate; capture per-step output after resume
content.activeForm: Watching workflow run and awaiting approval

content.subject: Task 1 — Live-state recon
content.description: Read-only gh api / npm view / git / file probes to confirm baseline state intact before dispatch (branch HEAD, release env, TP, package, npm, CHANGELOG, tags)

content.subject: Task 6 — Immediate cleanup (revoke allow-list, revert any temp trigger)

content.text:
Defect found — `npm publish --dry-run` fails with "You must specify a tag using `--tag` when publishing a prerelease version". The Story 3.1 D2 patch did the dry-run without the same `--tag` logic the real publish step has, so it breaks for every prerelease.

Fix: mirror the publish step's case-chain in the dry-run. Version slot still free (`Commit version bump` never ran, so nothing pushed to origin; `0.10.1-alpha.0` not burned).

input.old_string:
- name: Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: npm publish --dry-run
input.new_string:
- name: Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then TAG=alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then TAG=beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then TAG=rc
          else                                     TAG=latest
          fi
          npm publish --dry-run --tag "$TAG"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Release

# Canonical OIDC trusted-publishing release workflow for bmad-module-skill-forge.
# Consolidates publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml. Gated on the `release` GitHub
# Environment (required-reviewer approval). Filename MUST remain literal
# `release.yaml` — the npm Trusted Publisher is registered character-for-character
# on `release.yaml` + job id `release`. See docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version_bump:
        description: Version bump type
        required: true
        default: alpha
        type: choice
        options:
          - alpha
          - beta
          - rc
          - patch
          - minor
          - major
  push:
    branches: [feat/release-workflow]

permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: release
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: release
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm
          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org

      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: |
          echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
          # Side-prefix install — avoids the arborist rebuild race that bites
          # `npm install -g npm@latest` on Node 22.22.2's bundled npm 10.9.7
          # (old arborist cannot find its own promise-retry after the global
          # tree has been partially overwritten by the new npm mid-install).
          INSTALL_DIR="$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp"
          mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"
          npm install --prefix "$INSTALL_DIR" --no-save npm@latest
          echo "$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
          echo "Upgraded npm: $("$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin/npm" --version)"

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests and validation
        run: npm test

      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          BUMP="${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}"
          BUMP="${BUMP:-alpha}"
          case "$BUMP" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid="$BUMP" ;;
            *)             npm version "$BUMP" --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
        run: |
          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s

      - name: Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG
        run: |
          if grep -iE '(closes|fixes|resolves) \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md; then
            echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md contains non-numeric '(closes|fixes|resolves) [#...]' refs — aborting release."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: npm publish --dry-run

      - name: Commit version bump
        run: |
          git add package.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json CHANGELOG.md
          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: release_notes
        run: |
          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)
          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)
          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)

          {
            echo "## What's New"
            echo
            [ -n "$FEATURES" ] && { echo "### New Features"; echo "$FEATURES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$FIXES" ]    && { echo "### Bug Fixes"; echo "$FIXES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$OTHERS" ]   && { echo "### Other Changes"; echo "$OTHERS"; echo; }
            [ -n "$CHORES" ]   && { echo "### Maintenance"; echo "$CHORES"; echo; }
            echo "## Installation"
            echo
            echo '```bash'
            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
            echo '```'
            echo
            echo "**Full Changelog**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          } > release_notes.md

          {
            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
            cat release_notes.md
            echo "EOF"
          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create and push tag
        run: |
          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
          else
            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          fi

      - name: Push commit to main
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: git push origin HEAD:main

      - name: Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref)
        if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          echo "::notice::Dispatched from ${{ github.ref }} (not main); skipping 'git push origin HEAD:main'."
          echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} was pushed; main was NOT advanced."
          echo "This is the expected path for prerelease cuts from feature branches (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern)."

      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
          fi

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          {
            echo "## 🎉 Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo
            echo "**npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**GitHub**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

- name: Commit version bump

input.command:
git add .github/workflows/release.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(release): pass --tag in pre-publish dry-run to match real publish step

`npm publish --dry-run` without `--tag` errors out on prerelease versions:
  "You must specify a tag using --tag when publishing a prerelease version."

The real `Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing` step uses a case-chain
to pick alpha|beta|rc|latest based on the version string. The dry-run step
was missing that logic, so any prerelease cut (alpha/beta/rc) tripped the
dry-run before the real publish ever got a chance to fire.

Fix: duplicate the same case-chain in the dry-run step so it mirrors the
real publish step's tag selection. Non-prerelease versions still route to
`--tag latest` the same way.

This is a Story 3.1 D2 patch defect: the reviewer added the dry-run but
didn't carry the prerelease `--tag` handling over. Caught by Story 3.2's
third alpha-cut attempt — all prior steps green (npm upgrade fix held;
tests passed; bump/CHANGELOG/marketplace updates all ran clean); failure
surfaced specifically at the dry-run step.

Version slot for 0.10.1-alpha.0 remains unburned — Commit version bump,
tag push, and publish all never ran.

Refs: Story 3.2 Task 3 defect-fix cascade; third failed alpha-cut run
https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24714488585
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -8 && echo "===" && git push origin feat/release-workflow 2>&1 | tail -4

---
#!/usr/bin/env sh
[ "$HUSKY" = "2" ] && set -x
n=$(basename "$0")
s=$(dirname "$(dirname "$0")")/$n

[ ! -f "$s" ] && exit 0

if [ -f "$HOME/.huskyrc" ]; then
	echo "husky - '~/.huskyrc' is DEPRECATED, please move your code to ~/.config/husky/init.sh"
fi
i="${XDG_CONFIG_HOME:-$HOME/.config}/husky/init.sh"
[ -f "$i" ] && . "$i"

[ "${HUSKY-}" = "0" ] && exit 0

export PATH="node_modules/.bin:$PATH"
sh -e "$s" "$@"
c=$?

[ $c != 0 ] && echo "husky - $n script failed (code $c)"
[ $c = 127 ] && echo "husky - command not found in PATH=$PATH"
exit $c

content.text: Husky helper `.husky/_/h` natively checks `HUSKY=0` as a clean skip. That's husky's documented opt-out, not a `--no-verify` bypass. Setting `HUSKY: "0"` at the job level in `release.yaml` — scoped to CI only.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Release

# Canonical OIDC trusted-publishing release workflow for bmad-module-skill-forge.
# Consolidates publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml. Gated on the `release` GitHub
# Environment (required-reviewer approval). Filename MUST remain literal
# `release.yaml` — the npm Trusted Publisher is registered character-for-character
# on `release.yaml` + job id `release`. See docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version_bump:
        description: Version bump type
        required: true
        default: alpha
        type: choice
        options:
          - alpha
          - beta
          - rc
          - patch
          - minor
          - major
  push:
    branches: [feat/release-workflow]

permissions:
  contents: write
  id-token: write

concurrency:
  group: release
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: release
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
          cache: npm
          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org

      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
        run: |
          echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
          # Side-prefix install — avoids the arborist rebuild race that bites
          # `npm install -g npm@latest` on Node 22.22.2's bundled npm 10.9.7
          # (old arborist cannot find its own promise-retry after the global
          # tree has been partially overwritten by the new npm mid-install).
          INSTALL_DIR="$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp"
          mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"
          npm install --prefix "$INSTALL_DIR" --no-save npm@latest
          echo "$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
          echo "Upgraded npm: $("$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin/npm" --version)"

      - name: Install uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests and validation
        run: npm test

      - name: Configure Git
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Bump version
        run: |
          BUMP="${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}"
          BUMP="${BUMP:-alpha}"
          case "$BUMP" in
            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid="$BUMP" ;;
            *)             npm version "$BUMP" --no-git-tag-version ;;
          esac

      - name: Get new version and previous tag
        id: version
        run: |
          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Update marketplace.json version
        run: |
          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json

      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
        run: |
          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s

      - name: Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG
        run: |
          if grep -iE '(closes|fixes|resolves) \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md; then
            echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md contains non-numeric '(closes|fixes|resolves) [#...]' refs — aborting release."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then TAG=alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then TAG=beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then TAG=rc
          else                                     TAG=latest
          fi
          npm publish --dry-run --tag "$TAG"

      - name: Commit version bump
        run: |
          git add package.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json CHANGELOG.md
          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: release_notes
        run: |
          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)
          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)
          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)

          {
            echo "## What's New"
            echo
            [ -n "$FEATURES" ] && { echo "### New Features"; echo "$FEATURES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$FIXES" ]    && { echo "### Bug Fixes"; echo "$FIXES"; echo; }
            [ -n "$OTHERS" ]   && { echo "### Other Changes"; echo "$OTHERS"; echo; }
            [ -n "$CHORES" ]   && { echo "### Maintenance"; echo "$CHORES"; echo; }
            echo "## Installation"
            echo
            echo '```bash'
            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
            echo '```'
            echo
            echo "**Full Changelog**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          } > release_notes.md

          {
            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
            cat release_notes.md
            echo "EOF"
          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Create and push tag
        run: |
          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
          else
            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          fi

      - name: Push commit to main
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: git push origin HEAD:main

      - name: Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref)
        if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          echo "::notice::Dispatched from ${{ github.ref }} (not main); skipping 'git push origin HEAD:main'."
          echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} was pushed; main was NOT advanced."
          echo "This is the expected path for prerelease cuts from feature branches (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern)."

      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
        env:
          NPM_TOKEN: ""
        run: |
          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
          fi

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}
          name: "Skill Forge (SKF) v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
          body: ${{ steps.release_notes.outputs.RELEASE_NOTES }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'alpha') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'beta') || contains(steps.version.outputs.new_version, 'rc') }}

      - name: Summary
        run: |
          {
            echo "## 🎉 Released v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo
            echo "**npm**: https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**GitHub**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/releases/tag/v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
            echo "**Provenance**: auto-attached via OIDC trusted publishing"
          } >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

lines: release:
runs-on: ubuntu-latest
environment: release
+    env:
+      # Husky's documented opt-out (.husky/_/h checks this explicitly). Needed
+      # because .husky/commit-msg invokes `entire` (local dev session tool) and
+      # hard-exits if it's not on PATH — breaks CI's `Commit version bump`.
+      HUSKY: "0"
steps:
- name: Checkout
uses: actions/checkout@v4

input.command:
git add .github/workflows/release.yaml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(release): set HUSKY=0 job env to skip local git hooks in CI

The `Commit version bump` step invokes `git commit`, which fires .husky/
commit-msg, which hard-runs `entire hooks git commit-msg $1 || exit 1`.
`entire` is the maintainer's local session-tracking tool and is not
installed on the GitHub-hosted runner — the commit-msg hook exits 127
and blocks the bot's version-bump commit.

Husky ships a documented opt-out: .husky/_/h explicitly checks
`[ "${HUSKY-}" = "0" ] && exit 0` before invoking any hook. Setting
HUSKY=0 at the job level is husky's native, supported disable switch
— NOT a `git commit --no-verify` bypass. The local husky hooks remain
fully active for human contributors; only the bot-authored release
commit (ephemeral, tag-referenced, never pushed to feat branch HEAD)
skips them.

Scope is the release job; no other job env is affected. Alternative
considered — patching .husky/commit-msg to match the `|| true` pattern
used by the other 4 hooks in that directory — deferred to keep Story
3.2 scoped to release.yaml-only edits per AC #11 defect-protocol and
AC #12 scope envelope.

Refs: Story 3.2 Task 3 defect-fix cascade; fourth failed alpha-cut run
https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24714676318
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -8 && git push origin feat/release-workflow 2>&1 | tail -3

input.command: gh release view v0.10.1-alpha.0 --json body 2>&1 | jq -r '.body' | head -40

content.content:
## What's New

### New Features
- feat: add deterministic scoring script for test-skill completeness scoring
- feat: consolidate SKILL.md, add Python scripts, headless mode, and pipeline support
- feat: module-wide quality fixes — paths, triggers, provenance v2, output contracts
- feat: add deterministic frontmatter validator aligned with agentskills.io spec
- feat: add persistent workspace for remote source caching (Layer 0)
- feat(skf-create-skill): discovered authoritative files protocol (§2c)
- feat(skf-update-skill): mirror §2c into change detection (§1b)
- feat(health-check): announce arrival on entry for all chaining workflows
- feat(skf-create-skill): treat brief.version as implicit target_version
- feat(skf-export-skill): add snippet_skill_root_override config field
- feat(module-help): encode SKF workflow ordering in after/before fields
- feat(health-check): dedup submissions via deterministic fingerprint
- feat(shared): atomic-write helper + feasibility-report schema for v1.0
- feat(website): restructure sidebar into Why/Try/Reference buckets
- feat(releasing): create release GitHub environment with required-reviewer gate
- feat(releasing): register npm Trusted Publisher for release.yaml workflow
- feat(test-skill): add stackSkill typeCoverage skip condition
- feat(test-skill): skip §4b discovery test when catalog <2 skills
- feat(test-skill): pattern-ref branch in source-access-protocol
- feat(create-skill): add scope.type "reference-app"
- feat(brief-skill): extend scope.type reference-app through authoring path
- feat(release): author canonical release.yaml workflow
- feat(export-skill): define multi-skill execution semantics for --all

### Bug Fixes
- fix: resolve stale references, validator gaps, and documentation inconsistencies from skill migration
- fix: expand IDE mapping to all 23 IDEs with per-IDE skill root paths
- fix: replace stale platform terminology with context-file/IDE model across step files and docs
- fix: rename skf-setup-forge to skf-setup, move module files to assets/, fix CSV alignment
- fix: add missing hasSidecar field to core agent unit test
- fix: align CSV row ordering with forger capabilities table and populate args
- fix: restore module.yaml and module-help.csv to src/ root
- fix: expand bmad-synergy doc to reflect all 23 IDEs with per-IDE skill roots
- fix: correct three-case to four-case in export step-04 and declare zod devDependency
- fix: resolve 8 consistency issues found during commit review
- fix: resolve 5 issues found during commit review — encoding, regex, parser, docs, orphan ref
- fix: update 3 stale references to ephemeral-only clone terminology

1	name: Release
2	
3	# Canonical OIDC trusted-publishing release workflow for bmad-module-skill-forge.
4	# Consolidates publish.yaml + manual-release.yaml. Gated on the `release` GitHub
5	# Environment (required-reviewer approval). Filename MUST remain literal
6	# `release.yaml` — the npm Trusted Publisher is registered character-for-character
7	# on `release.yaml` + job id `release`. See docs/RELEASING.md § npm Trusted Publisher.
8	
9	on:
10	  workflow_dispatch:
11	    inputs:
12	      version_bump:
13	        description: Version bump type
14	        required: true
15	        default: alpha
16	        type: choice
17	        options:
18	          - alpha
19	          - beta
20	          - rc
21	          - patch
22	          - minor
23	          - major
24	
25	permissions:
26	  contents: write
27	  id-token: write
28	
29	concurrency:
30	  group: release
31	  cancel-in-progress: false
32	
33	jobs:
34	  release:
35	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
36	    environment: release
37	    env:
38	      # Husky's documented opt-out (.husky/_/h checks this explicitly). Needed
39	      # because .husky/commit-msg invokes `entire` (local dev session tool) and
40	      # hard-exits if it's not on PATH — breaks CI's `Commit version bump`.
41	      HUSKY: "0"
42	    steps:
43	      - name: Checkout
44	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
45	        with:
46	          fetch-depth: 0
47	          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
48	
49	      - name: Setup Node.js
50	        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
51	        with:
52	          node-version-file: ".nvmrc"
53	          cache: npm
54	          registry-url: https://registry.npmjs.org
55	
56	      - name: Ensure npm CLI supports trusted publishing
57	        run: |
58	          echo "Node: $(node --version); bundled npm: $(npm --version)"
59	          # Side-prefix install — avoids the arborist rebuild race that bites
60	          # `npm install -g npm@latest` on Node 22.22.2's bundled npm 10.9.7
61	          # (old arborist cannot find its own promise-retry after the global
62	          # tree has been partially overwritten by the new npm mid-install).
63	          INSTALL_DIR="$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp"
64	          mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"
65	          npm install --prefix "$INSTALL_DIR" --no-save npm@latest
66	          echo "$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
67	          echo "Upgraded npm: $("$INSTALL_DIR/node_modules/.bin/npm" --version)"
68	
69	      - name: Install uv
70	        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v6
71	        with:
72	          python-version: "3.12"
73	
74	      - name: Install dependencies
75	        run: npm ci
76	
77	      - name: Run tests and validation
78	        run: npm test
79	
80	      - name: Configure Git
81	        run: |
82	          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
83	          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
84	
85	      - name: Bump version
86	        run: |
87	          case "${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }}" in
88	            alpha|beta|rc) npm version prerelease --no-git-tag-version --preid=${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} ;;
89	            *)             npm version ${{ github.event.inputs.version_bump }} --no-git-tag-version ;;
90	          esac
91	
92	      - name: Get new version and previous tag
93	        id: version
94	        run: |
95	          echo "new_version=$(node -p "require('./package.json').version")" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
96	          echo "previous_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
97	
98	      - name: Update marketplace.json version
99	        run: |
100	          sed -i 's/"version": "[^"]*"/"version": "${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"/' .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
101	
102	      - name: Update CHANGELOG.md
103	        run: |
104	          npx conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -i CHANGELOG.md -s
105	
106	      - name: Assert no bogus issue refs in CHANGELOG
107	        run: |
108	          if grep -iE '(closes|fixes|resolves) \[#[^0-9]' CHANGELOG.md; then
109	            echo "::error::CHANGELOG.md contains non-numeric '(closes|fixes|resolves) [#...]' refs — aborting release."
110	            exit 1
111	          fi
112	
113	      - name: Pre-publish dry-run (catch package validation failures before tag push)
114	        env:
115	          NPM_TOKEN: ""
116	        run: |
117	          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
118	          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then TAG=alpha
119	          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then TAG=beta
120	          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then TAG=rc
121	          else                                     TAG=latest
122	          fi
123	          npm publish --dry-run --tag "$TAG"
124	
125	      - name: Commit version bump
126	        run: |
127	          git add package.json .claude-plugin/marketplace.json CHANGELOG.md
128	          git commit -m "release: bump to v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
129	
130	      - name: Generate release notes
131	        id: release_notes
132	        run: |
133	          COMMITS=$(git log ${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}..HEAD --pretty=format:"- %s" --reverse)
134	          FEATURES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (feat|Feature)" || true)
135	          FIXES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (fix|Fix)" || true)
136	          CHORES=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -E "^- (chore|Chore)" || true)
137	          OTHERS=$(echo "$COMMITS" | grep -v -E "^- (feat|Feature|fix|Fix|chore|Chore|release:|Release:)" || true)
138	
139	          {
140	            echo "## What's New"
141	            echo
142	            [ -n "$FEATURES" ] && { echo "### New Features"; echo "$FEATURES"; echo; }
143	            [ -n "$FIXES" ]    && { echo "### Bug Fixes"; echo "$FIXES"; echo; }
144	            [ -n "$OTHERS" ]   && { echo "### Other Changes"; echo "$OTHERS"; echo; }
145	            [ -n "$CHORES" ]   && { echo "### Maintenance"; echo "$CHORES"; echo; }
146	            echo "## Installation"
147	            echo
148	            echo '```bash'
149	            echo "npx bmad-module-skill-forge install"
150	            echo '```'
151	            echo
152	            echo "**Full Changelog**: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/compare/${{ steps.version.outputs.previous_tag }}...v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
153	          } > release_notes.md
154	
155	          {
156	            echo "RELEASE_NOTES<<EOF"
157	            cat release_notes.md
158	            echo "EOF"
159	          } >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
160	
161	      - name: Create and push tag
162	        run: |
163	          if git rev-parse "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
164	            echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} already exists, skipping tag creation"
165	          else
166	            git tag -a "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}" -m "Release v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
167	            git push origin "v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
168	          fi
169	
170	      - name: Push commit to main
171	        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
172	        run: git push origin HEAD:main
173	
174	      - name: Skip main push (non-main dispatch ref)
175	        if: github.ref != 'refs/heads/main'
176	        run: |
177	          echo "::notice::Dispatched from ${{ github.ref }} (not main); skipping 'git push origin HEAD:main'."
178	          echo "Tag v${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }} was pushed; main was NOT advanced."
179	          echo "This is the expected path for prerelease cuts from feature branches (Story 3.2 alpha-cut pattern)."
180	
181	      - name: Publish to npm via OIDC trusted publishing
182	        env:
183	          NPM_TOKEN: ""
184	        run: |
185	          VERSION="${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}"
186	          if   [[ "$VERSION" == *"alpha"* ]]; then npm publish --tag alpha
187	          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"beta"*  ]]; then npm publish --tag beta
188	          elif [[ "$VERSION" == *"rc"*    ]]; then npm publish --tag rc
189	          else                                     npm publish --tag latest
190	          fi
191


input.new_string:
### Completion Notes List

**Outcome:** Story 3.2 alpha cut succeeded end-to-end on run `24714953668`. The full OIDC trusted-publishing chain (publish → provenance → dist-tag routing → GitHub Release) is operationally proven against real infrastructure. Story 3.3 is now unblocked.

- **Published version:** `bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0` — https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/0.10.1-alpha.0 (permanent npm artifact; PRESERVED per AC #9).
- **Tag:** `v0.10.1-alpha.0` on origin, pointing at orphaned commit `2a57dcbd` (the CI-ephemeral `release: bump to v0.10.1-alpha.0`, reachable only via this tag per Story 3.1's `Skip main push` design).
- **GitHub Release:** https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/releases/tag/v0.10.1-alpha.0 (`prerelease: true`).
- **Workflow run:** https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24714953668 (22/22 steps green, 4 min 3 sec + manual approval latency).

**Dispatch mechanism:** Fallback path taken (temp `on: push: branches: [feat/release-workflow]` trigger + default-alpha bump logic in commit `e470501`). Primary `gh workflow run --ref` 404'd exactly as Epic 1 Story 1.2 precedent predicted — confirms GitHub Actions still requires `workflow_dispatch` workflows to exist on the default branch. The temp trigger was reverted at Task 6 via `7208ff4`; final `feat/release-workflow` HEAD carries only the 3 intentional defect-fix commits beyond `34ad130`.

**Defects caught + remediation (4 load-bearing patches, all on `feat/release-workflow`):**

1. **`f449c67`** — `fix(release): force npm self-upgrade to survive arborist rebuild race` (first attempt with `--force`; insufficient, superseded by `cbc7851` — kept in history per AC #11 "no amend, no force-push" rule).
2. **`cbc7851`** — `fix(release): side-prefix npm install to dodge self-upgrade rebuild race`. Root cause: `npm install -g npm@latest` on Node 22.22.2's bundled npm 10.9.7 crashes during arborist rebuild because the install partially overwrites `/opt/hostedtoolcache/.../lib/node_modules/npm/` before rebuild.js reads `promise-retry` from the now-partial tree. Fix: install to `$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp` side prefix, prepend `/node_modules/.bin` to `$GITHUB_PATH` — the old npm's tree stays intact, and subsequent steps resolve to the new npm.
3. **`de2334b`** — `fix(release): pass --tag in pre-publish dry-run to match real publish step`. Root cause: Story 3.1's D2 review patch added `npm publish --dry-run` without the tag-selection case-chain the real publish step uses; prerelease versions hit `You must specify a tag using --tag`. Fix: duplicate the exact case-chain in the dry-run step so both paths use identical logic.
4. **`cae5861`** — `fix(release): set HUSKY=0 job env to skip local git hooks in CI`. Root cause: `.husky/commit-msg` hard-runs `entire hooks git commit-msg $1 || exit 1`; `entire` is a local maintainer tool not installed on the runner, so bot's `Commit version bump` step died with exit 127. Fix: set `HUSKY: "0"` at job level — husky's documented opt-out (`.husky/_/h:L12` explicitly checks this). Not a `--no-verify` bypass; human contributors' hooks still fire normally.

**CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle finding (AC #10):** `conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -s` prepends the new release entry at byte-0 of the infile, pushing the `# Changelog` title + preamble + `[Unreleased]` anchor down to line 118+. Shape is now `[new release]...[Changelog title][preamble][Unreleased anchor][older releases]` — functionally broken as a Keep-a-Changelog document. **Flagged for Story 3.3 merge PR and/or Story 5.1 pre-v1.0.0 audit** to add a post-generation preamble-restore step (or switch the generation approach to honor the anchor as the insertion point). This is the first release-cut evidence supporting `deferred-work.md § "## [Unreleased] anchor lifecycle after first release is unverified"`.

**POLICY_ID revoke confirmation:** `47558675` created at Task 2 (POST) and deleted at Task 6 (DELETE). Final `gh api .../deployment-branch-policies` returns `[{id:47435919, name:"main"}]` only — baseline restored. Security window was open ~31 minutes.

**Scope-envelope confirmation:** `git diff 34ad130 feat/release-workflow -- .github/workflows/release.yaml` shows exactly 3 intentional hunks (HUSKY env var, side-prefix install, dry-run --tag) — no temp trigger residue, no drift. No other repo files were touched by Story 3.2.

**Orphaned-commit invariant check:** `git branch --contains v0.10.1-alpha.0` → empty. `package.json` on `feat/release-workflow` HEAD still reads `"version": "0.10.0"`. CI's bump mutation lives only in the tag-referenced `2a57dcbd` commit, as designed.

**Downstream-story signals flagged:**

- **Story 3.3 (merge PR)**: must carry `cbc7851`, `de2334b`, `cae5861` to main (all 3 are load-bearing for ANY release cut; removing them re-introduces proven defects). Additionally, Story 3.3 should consider bundling the CHANGELOG-preamble-restore patch per AC #10 finding, so that the FIRST real main-branch release doesn't ship with the malformed shape.
- **Story 5.1 (pre-v1.0.0 audit)**: (a) hand-patch CHANGELOG to restore proper Keep-a-Changelog shape before v1.0.0, OR rely on Story 3.3 having already added the workflow-level restore step; (b) review the `closes [#11]` / `closes [#12]` cosmetic refs in the CHANGELOG (these came from the dev agent writing `AC #11` / `AC #12` in commit bodies — the preset interpreted `#N` as GitHub issue refs; they point at whatever issues #11/#12 happen to be in the repo, likely unrelated).
- **Story 5.2 (v1.0.0-rc.1 cut)**: the full alpha-cut pattern proven here applies directly; Story 5.2 adds the one-time package.json hand-bump to `1.0.0-rc.0` before `version_bump: rc` per `deferred-work.md § 3-1-review "Story 5.2 needs a one-time hand-bump path"`. No additional workflow changes needed beyond the 3 Story 3.2 defect-fixes landing to main.

toolUseResult.newString:
### Completion Notes List

**Outcome:** Story 3.2 alpha cut succeeded end-to-end on run `24714953668`. The full OIDC trusted-publishing chain (publish → provenance → dist-tag routing → GitHub Release) is operationally proven against real infrastructure. Story 3.3 is now unblocked.

- **Published version:** `bmad-module-skill-forge@0.10.1-alpha.0` — https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/0.10.1-alpha.0 (permanent npm artifact; PRESERVED per AC #9).
- **Tag:** `v0.10.1-alpha.0` on origin, pointing at orphaned commit `2a57dcbd` (the CI-ephemeral `release: bump to v0.10.1-alpha.0`, reachable only via this tag per Story 3.1's `Skip main push` design).
- **GitHub Release:** https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/releases/tag/v0.10.1-alpha.0 (`prerelease: true`).
- **Workflow run:** https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/actions/runs/24714953668 (22/22 steps green, 4 min 3 sec + manual approval latency).

**Dispatch mechanism:** Fallback path taken (temp `on: push: branches: [feat/release-workflow]` trigger + default-alpha bump logic in commit `e470501`). Primary `gh workflow run --ref` 404'd exactly as Epic 1 Story 1.2 precedent predicted — confirms GitHub Actions still requires `workflow_dispatch` workflows to exist on the default branch. The temp trigger was reverted at Task 6 via `7208ff4`; final `feat/release-workflow` HEAD carries only the 3 intentional defect-fix commits beyond `34ad130`.

**Defects caught + remediation (4 load-bearing patches, all on `feat/release-workflow`):**

1. **`f449c67`** — `fix(release): force npm self-upgrade to survive arborist rebuild race` (first attempt with `--force`; insufficient, superseded by `cbc7851` — kept in history per AC #11 "no amend, no force-push" rule).
2. **`cbc7851`** — `fix(release): side-prefix npm install to dodge self-upgrade rebuild race`. Root cause: `npm install -g npm@latest` on Node 22.22.2's bundled npm 10.9.7 crashes during arborist rebuild because the install partially overwrites `/opt/hostedtoolcache/.../lib/node_modules/npm/` before rebuild.js reads `promise-retry` from the now-partial tree. Fix: install to `$RUNNER_TEMP/npm-tp` side prefix, prepend `/node_modules/.bin` to `$GITHUB_PATH` — the old npm's tree stays intact, and subsequent steps resolve to the new npm.
3. **`de2334b`** — `fix(release): pass --tag in pre-publish dry-run to match real publish step`. Root cause: Story 3.1's D2 review patch added `npm publish --dry-run` without the tag-selection case-chain the real publish step uses; prerelease versions hit `You must specify a tag using --tag`. Fix: duplicate the exact case-chain in the dry-run step so both paths use identical logic.
4. **`cae5861`** — `fix(release): set HUSKY=0 job env to skip local git hooks in CI`. Root cause: `.husky/commit-msg` hard-runs `entire hooks git commit-msg $1 || exit 1`; `entire` is a local maintainer tool not installed on the runner, so bot's `Commit version bump` step died with exit 127. Fix: set `HUSKY: "0"` at job level — husky's documented opt-out (`.husky/_/h:L12` explicitly checks this). Not a `--no-verify` bypass; human contributors' hooks still fire normally.

**CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle finding (AC #10):** `conventional-changelog-cli -p conventionalcommits -s` prepends the new release entry at byte-0 of the infile, pushing the `# Changelog` title + preamble + `[Unreleased]` anchor down to line 118+. Shape is now `[new release]...[Changelog title][preamble][Unreleased anchor][older releases]` — functionally broken as a Keep-a-Changelog document. **Flagged for Story 3.3 merge PR and/or Story 5.1 pre-v1.0.0 audit** to add a post-generation preamble-restore step (or switch the generation approach to honor the anchor as the insertion point). This is the first release-cut evidence supporting `deferred-work.md § "## [Unreleased] anchor lifecycle after first release is unverified"`.

**POLICY_ID revoke confirmation:** `47558675` created at Task 2 (POST) and deleted at Task 6 (DELETE). Final `gh api .../deployment-branch-policies` returns `[{id:47435919, name:"main"}]` only — baseline restored. Security window was open ~31 minutes.

**Scope-envelope confirmation:** `git diff 34ad130 feat/release-workflow -- .github/workflows/release.yaml` shows exactly 3 intentional hunks (HUSKY env var, side-prefix install, dry-run --tag) — no temp trigger residue, no drift. No other repo files were touched by Story 3.2.

**Orphaned-commit invariant check:** `git branch --contains v0.10.1-alpha.0` → empty. `package.json` on `feat/release-workflow` HEAD still reads `"version": "0.10.0"`. CI's bump mutation lives only in the tag-referenced `2a57dcbd` commit, as designed.

**Downstream-story signals flagged:**

- **Story 3.3 (merge PR)**: must carry `cbc7851`, `de2334b`, `cae5861` to main (all 3 are load-bearing for ANY release cut; removing them re-introduces proven defects). Additionally, Story 3.3 should consider bundling the CHANGELOG-preamble-restore patch per AC #10 finding, so that the FIRST real main-branch release doesn't ship with the malformed shape.
- **Story 5.1 (pre-v1.0.0 audit)**: (a) hand-patch CHANGELOG to restore proper Keep-a-Changelog shape before v1.0.0, OR rely on Story 3.3 having already added the workflow-level restore step; (b) review the `closes [#11]` / `closes [#12]` cosmetic refs in the CHANGELOG (these came from the dev agent writing `AC #11` / `AC #12` in commit bodies — the preset interpreted `#N` as GitHub issue refs; they point at whatever issues #11/#12 happen to be in the repo, likely unrelated).
- **Story 5.2 (v1.0.0-rc.1 cut)**: the full alpha-cut pattern proven here applies directly; Story 5.2 adds the one-time package.json hand-bump to `1.0.0-rc.0` before `version_bump: rc` per `deferred-work.md § 3-1-review "Story 5.2 needs a one-time hand-bump path"`. No additional workflow changes needed beyond the 3 Story 3.2 defect-fixes landing to main.
- **Process note** (Story 3.2 retro input): **Do not use `#N` as shorthand for AC numbers in commit bodies** — the conventional-commits preset parses any `#N` as a GitHub issue reference. Future stories should write `AC N`, `AC-11`, `acceptance criterion 11`, etc., to avoid polluting CHANGELOG with phantom `closes #N` links.

**Known unknown resolved:** `workflow_dispatch --ref <non-default>` still 404s on GitHub's current API. The fallback push-trigger pattern remains the operational workaround for feature-branch validation cuts. Epic 1 Story 1.2 precedent held exactly.

**Execution-quality note:** The dev agent implicitly authorized 4 separate user-approval clicks at the env gate during defect-fix iteration. In a non-solo-maintainer context (post Epic 1 Carry-forward #3 when `prevent_self_review` flips to `true`), each failed run would require the second reviewer's re-approval — materially increasing Story 3.2 cycle time. Documenting as a real-cost data point for Story 5.1's NFR1 (15-min CI) re-evaluation.

input.old_string:
### Review Findings

<!-- Post-execution code review (Blind Hunter / Edge Case Hunter / Acceptance Auditor) populates this.
     Expected lenses:
     - Blind Hunter: OIDC surface (provenance payload shape, TP binding drift), dist-tag case sensitivity, env-gate race conditions, post-approval re-trigger surface
     - Edge Case Hunter: version-string substring collisions (what if a future version is `1.0.0-march.1`?), concurrency-group collisions with the inert `publish.yaml`, `[Unreleased]` second-run behavior
     - Acceptance Auditor: ACs #1–#14 line-by-line against evidence in Debug Log References
-->
input.new_string:
### Review Findings

**Dev-agent-surfaced findings (pre-empting formal code review):**

1. **[HIGH] CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle is broken by `conventional-changelog-cli -s` append mode.** The preset prepends the new release entry at byte 0 of the infile, pushing `# Changelog` title (line 118), preamble (line 120), and `## [Unreleased]` anchor (line 122) BELOW the new release entry. Every subsequent release compounds the disorder. AC #10 answers Q1/Q2/Q3 documented in Debug Log References above. **Propose patch path to Story 3.3 merge PR or Story 5.1 audit** — add `.github/workflows/release.yaml` post-generation step that moves the preamble block (first `# Changelog` heading through first `## [Unreleased]` line) back to the top of the file. Alternative: switch generation approach.

2. **[MED] Dev agent wrote `AC #11` / `AC #12` in commit bodies; preset interpreted `#N` as GitHub issue refs.** The resulting CHANGELOG contains `closes [#11]` / `closes [#12]` links pointing at whatever issues bear those numbers in the repo (likely unrelated). The `Assert no bogus issue refs` guard correctly did NOT fire because these refs are numeric — the guard targets non-numeric refs (the Story 2.2 class-of-bug). Recommended process fix for all future Story dev agents: **write `AC N`, `AC-N`, or `acceptance criterion N` in commit bodies** — never `AC #N`. Story 5.1 audit can hand-correct the `0.10.1-alpha.0` CHANGELOG entry, or leave as historical artifact since the alpha is explicitly throwaway (AC #9 preserves it only as evidence).

3. **[LOW] `f449c67` (first failed fix attempt) is dead code in the commit chain.** The `--force` approach was superseded by `cbc7851` (side-prefix install). Preserving per AC #11 "no amend, no force-push" rule. Story 3.3 merge PR will carry it as noise in the commit-list diff view. Acceptable cost for audit-trail integrity; no action needed beyond reviewer awareness.

4. **[LOW] GitHub-hosted runner's bundled npm (Node 22.22.2 → npm 10.9.7) falls below the trusted-publisher floor of 11.5.1.** Documented in `cbc7851` commit body. The side-prefix install works but adds ~15s to CI time per run. Future optimization: re-check whether newer Node 22.x releases ship with npm ≥11.5.1 by default, then conditionally skip the bootstrap step. Not blocking; documented for Story 5.1's NFR1 cycle-time analysis.

5. **[INFO] Node.js 20 deprecation warning on every run.** Actions `actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-node@v4`, `astral-sh/setup-uv@v6`, and `softprops/action-gh-release@v2` are flagged as "running on Node.js 20" by the runner. GitHub will force Node 24 by 2026-06-02; Node 20 removed 2026-09-16. Two options: (a) rely on action maintainers to publish Node-24-compatible versions before the deadline (most have), (b) set `FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true` at workflow level. **Not a Story 3.2 concern** (the warnings fired on `feat/release-workflow` but also on `main` — this is a repo-wide upgrade scope). Flag for a future post-v1.0.0 maintenance story.

6. **[INFO] `uv` setup action emits cache-miss warning every run.** "No file matched to ... requirements.txt ... pyproject.toml ... uv.lock". The repo has Python files (test:python subcommand uses pytest via uv) but no lockfile, so the uv cache never fills. Release.yaml still runs `npm test` successfully (Python tests included). Low priority; maintenance cleanup for a future story.

**Deferred to formal review:**

- Blind Hunter lens on OIDC surface (attestation payload shape at `.dist.attestations`, TP binding drift risk)
- Edge Case Hunter on version-string substring collisions (e.g., a future `1.0.0-march.1` would match `*"rc"*` in the case-chain — Story 3.1 code-review patch D didn't flag this; worth a second look)
- Acceptance Auditor line-by-line AC #1–#14 against Debug Log References evidence

content.description: npm view dist-tags, provenance jq verification, gh release view, CHANGELOG [Unreleased] lifecycle diff + three-question answers

content.description: Read-only gh api / npm view / git / file probes to confirm baseline state intact before dispatch (branch HEAD, release env, TP, package, npm, CHANGELOG, tags)

# STATUS DEFINITIONS:
# ==================
# Epic Status:
#   - backlog: Epic not yet started
#   - in-progress: Epic actively being worked on
#   - done: All stories in epic completed
#
# Epic Status Transitions:
#   - backlog → in-progress: Automatically when first story is created (via create-story)
#   - in-progress → done: Manually when all stories reach 'done' status
#
# Story Status:
#   - backlog: Story only exists in epic file
#   - ready-for-dev: Story file created in stories folder
#   - in-progress: Developer actively working on implementation
#   - review: Ready for code review (via Dev's code-review workflow)
#   - done: Story completed
#
# Retrospective Status:
#   - optional: Can be completed but not required
#   - done: Retrospective has been completed
#
# WORKFLOW NOTES:
# ===============
# - Epic transitions to 'in-progress' automatically when first story is created
# - Stories can be worked in parallel if team capacity allows
# - Developer typically creates next story after previous one is 'done' to incorporate learnings
# - Dev moves story to 'review', then runs code-review (fresh context, different LLM recommended)

content.text:
## Story 3.2 complete — Status: `review`

**First-ever OIDC trusted publish of `bmad-module-skill-forge` succeeded.** Run `24714953668`, all 22 steps green on the fifth attempt after four live-validator-surfaced defect fixes. Every AC verified.

### Published artifacts (permanent)

- **npm:** https://www.npmjs.com/package/bmad-module-skill-forge/v/0.10.1-alpha.0 — dist-tag `alpha`, `latest` unchanged at `0.10.0` (default-`latest` trap avoided), SLSA Provenance v1.0 attestation attached and verified via `npm audit signatures`.
- **Tag:** `v0.10.1-alpha.0` on origin (points at orphaned CI bump commit `2a57dcbd`).
- **GitHub Release:** https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge/releases/tag/v0.10.1-alpha.0 — `prerelease: true`.

### Load-bearing workflow fixes staying on `feat/release-workflow` (Story 3.3 will carry them to main)

- `cbc7851` — side-prefix npm upgrade to dodge bundled-npm self-upgrade race (Node 22.22.2 ships npm 10.9.7, below the 11.5.1 TP floor)
- `de2334b` — `--tag` flag in pre-publish dry-run (prerelease validation was broken)
- `cae5861` — `HUSKY: "0"` job env (husky's own documented opt-out, not a `--no-verify` bypass)

Temp push-trigger (`e470501`) reverted by `7208ff4`. Final diff vs `34ad130` is exactly these three hunks — scope envelope clean.

### Findings flagged for downstream

- **[HIGH] CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` anchor lifecycle is broken.** `conventional-changelog-cli -s` prepends at byte 0, pushing `# Changelog` title + preamble + `[Unreleased]` anchor down to line 118. Every release compounds it. Proposed patch (add a preamble-restore step to `release.yaml`, or switch generation approach) → Story 3.3 merge PR or Story 5.1 audit.
- **[MED] Process note** — don't write `AC #N` in commit bodies (the conventionalcommits preset parses `#N` as GH issue refs → CHANGELOG has phantom `closes [#11]` / `closes [#12]` links).
- **[LOW]** `f449c67` (first failed `--force` attempt) is superseded but preserved in history per AC #11's "no amend / no force-push" rule.

### Env security state

Allow-list widened at 09:08 GMT+4 (`POLICY_ID=47558675`), revoked at 09:40. Window open ≈ 31 min. Current state: `[{id: 47435919, name: "main"}]` only.

### Story file

`_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/3-2-validate-release-yaml-via-alpha-test-cut-from-feature-branch.md` — Status: `review`. Dev Agent Record fully populated with AC #13's evidence block + self-flagged review findings. Gitignored per BMAD convention; local-only.

### Suggested next steps

1. **Run `code-review` against this story** using a *different* LLM than me (per workflow tip). Blind Hunter + Edge Case Hunter lenses are especially relevant for the OIDC surface (TP binding drift, attestation payload shape, version-string substring collisions like a future `1.0.0-march.1` matching `*"rc"*`).
2. **Decide the CHANGELOG preamble fix** — should I add the restore step to `release.yaml` in a follow-up Story 3.2 commit (expands scope) or defer to Story 3.3's merge PR? My preference is defer, since Story 3.2's scope is live-validator only.
3. **Move on to Story 3.3** — merge `feat/release-workflow` → `main` with the atomic `publish.yaml` tag-trigger neutralization per Epic 1 carry-forward #2.

Want me to explain any of the defect fixes in more depth, run a mental code-review pass, or just stop here?
